## Thread: Problem with linear search on an internal table — 15 msgs

### Problem with linear search on an internal table

- **From:** hornedw@yahoo.com (David Horne)
- **Date:** 2002-11-01T16:46:40-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<b9d3f3a8.0211011646.f1b6f98@posting.google.com>`

```
I was wondering if anybody could help me with a cobol program that I
am working on. The problem that I am having is a particular field in
which I have to display a rating. In the file where the records are
located, there are the number 0,1, and 2. These numbers corrospond to
the following ratings 0 = DISSATISFIED, 1 =UNDECIDED, and 
2=SATISFIED. In the report that I am displaying, the ratings must be
displayed as DISS, UND, AND SAT. I get these abbreviations from an
internal(embedded) table . I am suppose to perform a linear search for
these ratings. When I run it, I get a run-time error saying  that I
have an illegal character in a numeric field. The code that is for the
table is below:

WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.

       01 RATING-TABLE-DATA.
           02                                  PIC X(5) VALUE "0DISS".
           02                                  PIC X(5) VALUE "1UND ".
           02                                  PIC X(5) VALUE "2SAT ".
       01 RATING-TABLE REDEFINES RATING-TABLE-DATA.
           02 RT-RATING OCCURS 73 TIMES INDEXED BY RT-INDEX.
               03 RT-CODE                      PIC X.
               03 RT-WORD                      PIC X(4).

PROCEDURE DIVISION.
           SET RT-INDEX TO 1.
           SEARCH RT-RATING
               WHEN RT-CODE(RT-INDEX) = CR-SATISFACTION-RATING
                   MOVE RT-WORD(RT-INDEX) TO WA-RATING.
```

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DC35CBF.3090807@Sympatico.ca>`
- **References:** `<b9d3f3a8.0211011646.f1b6f98@posting.google.com>`

```
I notice that the table is 3 elements long, but the redefinition is 73 
long.  That a typo, or did
you just not want to post the other 70 lines of  data?  My compiler 
would not have compiled
the below, as it is an illegal re-definition.  The redefinition area 
cannot be larger than the area
redefined.

Donald

David Horne wrote:

>I was wondering if anybody could help me with a cobol program that I
>am working on. The problem that I am having is a particular field in
…[27 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Problem with linear search on an internal table

- **From:** Liam Devlin <LiamD@optonline.NOSPAM.net>
- **Date:** 2002-11-02T06:29:06+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DC37081.7020309@optonline.NOSPAM.net>`
- **References:** `<b9d3f3a8.0211011646.f1b6f98@posting.google.com>`

```
David Horne wrote:
> I was wondering if anybody could help me with a cobol program that I
> am working on. The problem that I am having is a particular field in
…[25 more quoted lines elided]…
>                    MOVE RT-WORD(RT-INDEX) TO WA-RATING.

Any possibility you have a record which has neither Zero, nor 1, nor 2? 
That would take you to the 4th occurrence, which is undefined in the 
code fragment posted. Why do you specify "73 times"?

For such a small number of options, I'd probably code an Evaluate with 3 
cases and be done with it, e.g.:

Evaluate	CR-SATISFACTION-RATING
When		Zero
	Move  "DISS"	to	WA-RATING
When		'1'
	Move  "UND"	to	WA-RATING

You have a choice here concerning the next case, if you make it '2', I'd 
also code an Other case to catch any records with bum rating codes, if 
that's possible. Otherwise, I'd code:

When		Other
	Move  "SAT"	to	WA-RATING

End-Evaluate

I see no point in coding a Search for such a small number of arguments.

YMMV
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Problem with linear search on an internal table

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)

- **References:** `<b9d3f3a8.0211011646.f1b6f98@posting.google.com> <3DC37081.7020309@optonline.NOSPAM.net>`

```
Liam Devlin <LiamD@optonline.NOSPAM.net> wrote
> 
> I see no point in coding a Search for such a small number of arguments.

It's homework, he is being taught to use tables and SEARCH.  It is
easier to get it working with 3 items pre-set in W-S than to get
bogged down in code necessary to dynamically create a real table of a
few hundred items, maybe next week they will move on to that.
```

```
>> I was wondering if anybody could help me with a cobol program that I
…[52 more quoted lines elided]…

I would dispute the use of Evaluate here over the table.  In case
other codes are added, you need only add to the table.  With Evaluate
you would have to add an instruction.

I suspect his problem is due to the definition though.
```

#### ↳ Re: Problem with linear search on an internal table

- **From:** einomoto@compuserve.com (Edmond Inomoto)
- **Date:** 2002-11-02T14:52:48+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<00021002095127.OUI53.einomoto@compuserve.com>`
- **References:** `<b9d3f3a8.0211011646.f1b6f98@posting.google.com>`

```
Hello,

On November 01 2002 at 16:46:40 -0800, hornedw@yahoo.com (David Horne) 
wrote:

> I was wondering if anybody could help me with a cobol program that I
> am working on. The problem that I am having is a particular field in
…[24 more quoted lines elided]…
> WHEN RT-CODE(RT-INDEX) = CR-SATISFACTION-RATING


Is a linear search required for some reason? For such a small number of
entries, I'd use direct access.

01  .
    05   RATING-TABLE           VALUE "DISSUND SAT ".
         10  RT-WORD OCCURS 3   PIC X(4).
     
PROCEDURE DIVISION.
MAINLINE.
    DISPLAY RT-WORD (CR-SATISFACTION-RATING + 1).


If you want to keep your basic structure, maybe changing RT-CODE from
PIC X to PIC 9 would help. RT-RATING should also have OCCURS 3, not 73.

HTH.

--  Edmond Inomoto
```

```
>
```

#### ↳ Re: Problem with linear search on an internal table

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2002-11-02T09:06:06-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0211020906.407b30a9@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<b9d3f3a8.0211011646.f1b6f98@posting.google.com>`

```
hornedw@yahoo.com (David Horne) wrote

> When I run it, I get a run-time error saying  that I
> have an illegal character in a numeric field. The code that is for the
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
>                03 RT-WORD                      PIC X(4).

None of these are numeric fields, so it is unrelated to these.
 
> PROCEDURE DIVISION.
>            SET RT-INDEX TO 1.
>            SEARCH RT-RATING
>                WHEN RT-CODE(RT-INDEX) = CR-SATISFACTION-RATING
>                    MOVE RT-WORD(RT-INDEX) TO WA-RATING.

We don't have the declaration for CR-SATISFACTION-RATING, I suspect
that it is a numeric field and has an actual value that is not formed
from numeric characters.

It would seem that this field is in an FD and you haven't READ a
record from the file into the record area, or if you have the record
in the file hasn't non-numeric characters in this position.
```

```
This is David. I have tried what everybody has mentioned but it still
does not work. I have posted the entire code minus the indentfication
division for the program below. I am still getting the same error.
This is a homework assignment, so I am required to use a linear search
on the satisfaction rating . The textbook for the class is not helpful
for this problem and the example we will do in class does not perform
a linear search on an internal(embedded) table.

ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
      **********************

       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
      **********************

       FILE-CONTROL.

           SELECT CUSTOMER-FILE
               ASSIGN TO "C:\COBOL\CUSTSALE.DAT".

           SELECT ADDRESS-FILE
               ASSIGN TO "C:\COBOL\CUSTADDR.DAT".

           SELECT SORT-FILE
               ASSIGN TO "SORTWORK".


      /
       DATA DIVISION.
      ***************

       FILE SECTION.
      **************

      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *    INPUT-FILE -     CUSTOMER SALES MASTER FILE                
*
      *                                                               
*
      *    CHARACTERISTICS: LAYOUT FOR FILE CUSTSALE.DAT              
*
      *                     FILE ORGANIZATION - SEQUENTIAL            
*
      *                     RECORD LENGTH     - 73 BYTES              
*
      ******************************************************************

       FD  CUSTOMER-FILE.

       01  CUSTOMER-REC                       PIC X(73).

      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *  CUSTOMER ADDRESS FILE FOR LOADING NON-EMBEDDED TABLE         
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       FD  ADDRESS-FILE.

       01  ADDRESS-RECORD.
           02  AR-CUSTOMER-NUMBER              PIC X(4).
           02  AR-CUSTOMER-ADDRESS             PIC X(20).

      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *           SORT FILE FOR SORTING CUSTOMER SALES FILE           
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************
       SD  SORT-FILE.

       01 SORT-RECORD.
           02                                  PIC X(41).
           02 SR-AUTO-MAKE                     PIC X(20).
           02                                  PIC X(15).
      /
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
      ************************

      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *                        SWITCHES                               
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       01  SWITCHES.

           02  SW-END-OF-FILE                  PIC X.
               88  END-OF-FILE                             VALUE "Y".

      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *                      ACCUMULATORS                             
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       01  ACCUMULATORS.

           02  AC-LINE-COUNT                   PIC 999.
           02  AC-PAGE-COUNT                   PIC 999.
           02  AC-RECORD-COUNT                 PIC 999.
           02  AC-TOTAL-PURCHASES              PIC 9(6)V99.
      /
      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *                       WORK AREA FIELDS                        
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       01  WORK-AREA.

           02  WA-TODAYS-DATE-TIME.
               03  WA-TODAYS-DATE.
                   04  WA-TODAYS-YEAR          PIC 9(4).
                   04  WA-TODAYS-MONTH         PIC 99.
                   04  WA-TODAYS-DAY           PIC 99.
               03  WA-TODAYS-TIME.
                   04  WA-TODAYS-HOUR          PIC 99.
                   04  WA-TODAYS-MINUTES       PIC 99.
               03                              PIC X(9).

           02  WA-AM-PM                        PIC XX.
           02  WA-SCREEN-HOLD                  PIC X.
           02  WA-HYPHENS                      PIC X(20) VALUE ALL
"-".
           02  WA-CUSTOMER-NUMBER              PIC X(4).
           02  WA-CUSTOMER-ADDRESS             PIC X(20).
           02  WA-RATING                       PIC X(4).

      /
      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *                NON-EMBEDDED CUSTOMER ADDRESS TABLE            
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       01  ADDRESS-TABLE.
           02  AT-ENTRY                       OCCURS 24 TIMES
                                              ASCENDING KEY
                                                    
AT-CUSTOMER-NUMBER
                                              INDEXED BY AT-INDEX.
               03  AT-CUSTOMER-NUMBER          PIC X(4).
               03  AT-CUSTOMER-ADDRESS         PIC X(20).

      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *                   EMBEDDED SATISFACTION RATING TABLE          
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       01  RATING-TABLE-DATA.
           02                                PIC X(5) VALUE "0DISS".
           02                                PIC X(5) VALUE "1UND".
           02                                PIC X(5) VALUE "2SAT".

       01  RATING-TABLE REDEFINES RATING-TABLE-DATA.
           02  RT-RATING OCCURS 3 TIMES INDEXED BY RT-INDEX.
               03 RATING-CODE                PIC X.
               03 RATING-WORD                PIC X(4).
      /
      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *    INPUT-FILE -     CUSTOMER SALES MASTER FILE RECORD LAYOUT  
*
      *                                                               
*
      *    CHARACTERISTICS: LAYOUT FOR FILE CUSTSALE.DAT              
*
      *                     FILE ORGANIZATION - SEQUENTIAL            
*
      *                     RECORD LENGTH     -  76 BYTES             
*
      *                                                               
*
      *                     DATE FORMAT       - YYYYMMDD              
*
      ******************************************************************

       01  CUSTOMER-RECORD.

           02  CR-CUSTOMER-ZIP-CODE            PIC X(5).
           02  CR-CUSTOMER-ZIP-PLUS4           PIC X(4).
           02  CR-CUSTOMER-NUMBER              PIC X(4).
           02  CR-CUSTOMER-NAME                PIC X(20).
           02  CR-PURCHASE-DATE                PIC 9(8).
           02  CR-AUTO-MAKE                    PIC X(20).
           02  CR-PURCHASE-PRICE               PIC 9(5)V99.
           02  CR-AUTO-YEAR                    PIC 9(4).
           02  FILLER                          PIC X(3).
           02  CR-SATISFACTION-RATING          PIC X.
               88 DISSATISFIED                 VALUE "0".
               88 UNDECIDED                    VALUE "1".
               88 SATISFIED                    VALUE "2".
      /
       SCREEN SECTION.
      ***************

      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *       REPORT HEADINGS FOR THE CUSTOMER ADDRESS REPORT         
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       01  HEADING-LINES.
           02  BLANK SCREEN.
           02        LINE 1 COLUMN 1  VALUE "DATE: ".
           02               COLUMN 7  PIC Z9 FROM WA-TODAYS-MONTH.
           02               COLUMN 9  VALUE "/".
           02               COLUMN 10 PIC 99   FROM WA-TODAYS-DAY.
           02               COLUMN 12 VALUE "/".
           02               COLUMN 13 PIC 9999 FROM WA-TODAYS-YEAR.
           02               COLUMN 32 VALUE "EZ AUTO SALES".
           02               COLUMN 72 VALUE "PAGE ".
           02               COLUMN 77 PIC ZZ9  FROM AC-PAGE-COUNT.
           02        LINE 2 COLUMN 1  VALUE "TIME: ".
           02               COLUMN 7  PIC Z9 FROM WA-TODAYS-HOUR.
           02               COLUMN 9  VALUE ":".
           02               COLUMN 10 PIC XX FROM WA-TODAYS-MINUTES.
           02               COLUMN 13 PIC XX FROM WA-AM-PM.
           02               COLUMN 27 VALUE "CUSTOMER ADDRESS REPORT".
           02        LINE 4 COLUMN 1  VALUE "CUSTOMER".
           02               COLUMN 22 VALUE "STREET".
           02               COLUMN 43 VALUE "AUTO".
           02               COLUMN 65 VALUE "PURCHASE".
           02        LINE 5 COLUMN 1  VALUE "NAME".
           02               COLUMN 22 VALUE "ADDRESS".
           02               COLUMN 43 VALUE "MAKE".
           02               COLUMN 65 VALUE "PRICE".
           02               COLUMN 76 VALUE "SAT.".
           02        LINE 6 COLUMN 1  PIC X(20) FROM WA-HYPHENS.
           02               COLUMN 22 PIC X(20) FROM WA-HYPHENS.
           02               COLUMN 43 PIC X(20) FROM WA-HYPHENS.
           02               COLUMN 65 PIC X(9)  FROM WA-HYPHENS.
           02               COLUMN 76 PIC X(4)  FROM WA-HYPHENS.
      /
      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *          DETAIL LINE FOR THE CUSTOMER ADDRESS REPORT          
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       01  DETAIL-LINE.

           02  LINE AC-LINE-COUNT.
           02            COLUMN 1  PIC X(20)  FROM CR-CUSTOMER-NAME.
           02            COLUMN 22 PIC X(20)  FROM
WA-CUSTOMER-ADDRESS.
           02            COLUMN 43 PIC X(20)  FROM CR-AUTO-MAKE.
           02            COLUMN 65 PIC ZZ,ZZ9.99 FROM
CR-PURCHASE-PRICE.
           02            COLUMN 76 PIC X(4) FROM WA-RATING.
      
      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *           SUMMARY LINE FOR THE CUSTOMER ADDRESS REPORT        
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       01  SUMMARY-LINES.

           02  SL-LINE-1.
               03  LINE AC-LINE-COUNT.
               03            COLUMN 22 VALUE
                   "TOTAL CUSTOMERS LISTED = ".
               03            COLUMN 57 PIC ZZ9 FROM AC-RECORD-COUNT.

           02  SL-LINE-2.
               03  LINE AC-LINE-COUNT.
               03            COLUMN 22 VALUE
                   "TOTAL CUSTOMER PURCHASES = ".
               03            COLUMN 49 PIC $ZZZ,ZZ9.99 FROM
                                       AC-TOTAL-PURCHASES.

           02  SL-LINE-3.
               03  LINE AC-LINE-COUNT.
               03            COLUMN 34 VALUE "END OF REPORT".
      /
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
      *******************
      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *                         MAIN PROGRAM                          
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       MAIN-PROGRAM.

           PERFORM A-100-INITIALIZATION.
           PERFORM B-100-LOAD-ADDRESS-TABLE.
           PERFORM C-100-PROCESS-FILE.
           PERFORM D-100-WRAP-UP.
           STOP RUN.

      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *                    INITIALIZATION PARAGRAPH                   
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       A-100-INITIALIZATION.

           INITIALIZE ACCUMULATORS.

           MOVE FUNCTION CURRENT-DATE TO WA-TODAYS-DATE-TIME.

           EVALUATE TRUE
               WHEN WA-TODAYS-HOUR = 00
                   MOVE "AM" TO WA-AM-PM
                   MOVE 12 TO WA-TODAYS-HOUR
               WHEN WA-TODAYS-HOUR < 12
                   MOVE "AM" TO WA-AM-PM
               WHEN WA-TODAYS-HOUR = 12
                   MOVE "PM" TO WA-AM-PM
               WHEN WA-TODAYS-HOUR > 12
                   MOVE "PM" TO WA-AM-PM
                   SUBTRACT 12 FROM WA-TODAYS-HOUR.
      /
      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *                    TABLE LOADING PARAGRAPH                    
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       B-100-LOAD-ADDRESS-TABLE.

           MOVE "N" TO SW-END-OF-FILE.
           OPEN   INPUT  ADDRESS-FILE.

           READ ADDRESS-FILE
               AT END MOVE "Y" TO SW-END-OF-FILE.

           PERFORM B-200-LOAD-LOCATION
               VARYING AT-INDEX FROM 1 BY 1
               UNTIL END-OF-FILE OR AT-INDEX > 24.

           CLOSE ADDRESS-FILE.

      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *           MOVE ADDRESSS FILE DATA TO ADDRESS TABLE            
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       B-200-LOAD-LOCATION.

           MOVE AR-CUSTOMER-NUMBER
                   TO AT-CUSTOMER-NUMBER(AT-INDEX).
           MOVE AR-CUSTOMER-ADDRESS TO AT-CUSTOMER-ADDRESS (AT-INDEX).

           READ ADDRESS-FILE
               AT END MOVE "Y" TO SW-END-OF-FILE.
      /
      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *             FILE PROCESSING CONTROL PARAGRAPH                 
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       C-100-PROCESS-FILE.

           SORT SORT-FILE
               ON ASCENDING KEY SR-AUTO-MAKE
               USING CUSTOMER-FILE
               GIVING CUSTOMER-FILE.

           OPEN INPUT   CUSTOMER-FILE.
           MOVE "N" TO SW-END-OF-FILE.
           READ CUSTOMER-FILE INTO CUSTOMER-RECORD
               AT END MOVE "Y" TO SW-END-OF-FILE.
           PERFORM C-200-PROCESS-RECORD
               UNTIL END-OF-FILE.
           ADD 1 TO AC-LINE-COUNT.
           DISPLAY SL-LINE-1.
           ADD 1 TO AC-LINE-COUNT.
           DISPLAY SL-LINE-2.
           ADD 1 TO AC-LINE-COUNT.
           DISPLAY SL-LINE-3.
      /
      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *          SEARCH TABLES AND DISPLAY DETAIL LINE                
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       C-200-PROCESS-RECORD.

           IF AC-LINE-COUNT = 0
               ADD 1 TO AC-PAGE-COUNT
               DISPLAY HEADING-LINES
               MOVE 6 TO AC-LINE-COUNT.

           SEARCH ALL AT-ENTRY
               AT END
                   MOVE "ADDR. NOT FOUND" TO WA-CUSTOMER-ADDRESS
               WHEN AT-CUSTOMER-NUMBER(AT-INDEX)
                       = CR-CUSTOMER-NUMBER
                   MOVE AT-CUSTOMER-ADDRESS(AT-INDEX) TO
                           WA-CUSTOMER-ADDRESS.


           SET RT-INDEX TO 1.
           SEARCH RT-RATING
               AT END
                   MOVE "NONE" TO WA-RATING
               WHEN RATING-CODE (RT-INDEX) = CR-SATISFACTION-RATING
                   MOVE RATING-WORD (RT-INDEX) TO WA-RATING.




           DISPLAY DETAIL-LINE.
           ADD 1 TO AC-LINE-COUNT.
           ADD 1 TO AC-RECORD-COUNT.

           IF AC-LINE-COUNT > 21
               DISPLAY " "
               DISPLAY "PLEASE PRESS THE ENTER KEY TO CONTINUE"
               ACCEPT WA-SCREEN-HOLD
               MOVE ZERO TO AC-LINE-COUNT.

           READ CUSTOMER-FILE INTO CUSTOMER-RECORD
               AT END MOVE "Y" TO SW-END-OF-FILE.
      /
      ******************************************************************
      *                                                               
*
      *                      END OF JOB PARAGRAPH                     
*
      *                                                               
*
      ******************************************************************

       D-100-WRAP-UP.
           DISPLAY " ".
           DISPLAY " ".
           DISPLAY "PLEASE PRESS THE ENTER KEY TO CONTINUE".
           ACCEPT WA-SCREEN-HOLD.
           CLOSE CUSTOMER-FILE.
           DISPLAY " ".
           DISPLAY "CUSTOMER ADDRESS REPORT PROGRAM HAS TERMINATED".
           DISPLAY " ".

      ******************************************************************
      *                         END OF PROGRAM                        
*
      ******************************************************************
      /
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Problem with linear search on an internal table

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-03T17:50:11-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aq4cno$a5u$1@slb7.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<b9d3f3a8.0211011646.f1b6f98@posting.google.com> <217e491a.0211020906.407b30a9@posting.google.com> <8a2d426e.0211021812.65e8b518@posting.google.com> <b9d3f3a8.0211031031.14680dea@posting.google.com>`

```
David,
  It is JUST A GUESS, but I see that you have Select/Assign statements of:

            SELECT CUSTOMER-FILE
                ASSIGN TO "C:\COBOL\CUSTSALE.DAT".

            SELECT ADDRESS-FILE
                ASSIGN TO "C:\COBOL\CUSTADDR.DAT".

From the ASSIGN clauses, my GUESS is that you are running on a PC or on Unix
or Linux.  If so (and you haven't told us what operating system or compiler
you are using), I have a GUESS that at least your input file (and possibly
your output file) are (or are supposed to be)

   LINE SEQUENTIAL

rather than just "sequential" (which is the default for ANSI conforming
compilers).

If so, it is possible that when you are reading your input records, you are
loosing or gaining CR/LF (carriage-return / line-feeds) and thereby getting
the wrong data in records after the first one.  To solve this problem, try
adding

  Organization is Line Sequential

to your Select/Assign statements.

To see if this really IS your problem, you might want to do a DISPLAY after
each (successful) READ and see if your records look the way you expect them
to.  (If not, when you do the DISPLAY with a FROM clause for a PIC 9 field,
you may get problems).

One other GENERAL question, you might want to find out what (if any)
interactive debugger comes with your compiler.  This would let you "step"
thru your program and see the value of variables and where exactly your
program is having a problem.  My guess is that it is NOT the SEARCH
statement, but something referencing a PIC 9 field.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Problem with linear search on an internal table

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2002-11-03T21:06:16-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0211032106.58d5bfd2@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<b9d3f3a8.0211011646.f1b6f98@posting.google.com> <217e491a.0211020906.407b30a9@posting.google.com> <8a2d426e.0211021812.65e8b518@posting.google.com> <b9d3f3a8.0211031031.14680dea@posting.google.com>`

```
hornedw@yahoo.com (David Horne) wrote

>       *    CHARACTERISTICS: LAYOUT FOR FILE CUSTSALE.DAT              
>       *                     FILE ORGANIZATION - SEQUENTIAL            
>       *                     RECORD LENGTH     - 73 BYTES              
>        FD  CUSTOMER-FILE.
>        01  CUSTOMER-REC                       PIC X(73).

>        01  CUSTOMER-RECORD.
>            02  CR-CUSTOMER-ZIP-CODE            PIC X(5).
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
>                88 SATISFIED                    VALUE "2".

This adds up to * 76 * characters.  The result of the implicit MOVE of
the 73 characters defined in CUSTOMER-REC to CUSTOMER-RECORD is that
the last 3 characters will be spaces, this is regardless of what the
actual file looks like.

CR-Satisfaction-Rating will never be anything other than a space.

I suspect that you had this field defined as PIC 9 when you had the
error in a numeric field - space is not a valid numeric character.

If this wasn't the case then look for another numeric field that may
have had an invalid character in it.
```
