###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Cobol MVS vs IMS

- **From:** Binyamin Dissen <postingid@dissensoftware.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-06T12:10:43+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2dqhsu0dqfv4tfckk4ha945p43b48ps5ah@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<6dc3887b.0211051815.223625fc@posting.google.com> <01c2853b$95acf520$168bf243@chottel> <aq9vuj$qo2$1@slb0.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
On Tue, 5 Nov 2002 20:48:48 -0600 "William M. Klein"
<wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com> wrote:

:>I slightly disagree with Charles' summary.

:> CICS (which the original poster knows) is ONLY a "transaction management
:>system".  It relies on various "file" systems (including VSAM, DB2 or even
:>IMS) for "data storage".

:>IMS includes two parts  - one is a hierarchical database system (which can
:>be accessed from batch, from CICS, *or* from an IMS transaction system).  In
:>addition, IMS provides its own  "transaction system"

:>    ***

:>From a COBOL programmer's point of view,  CICS uses
:>   EXEC CICS  (and/or EXEC DLI and/or EXEC SQL) statements to do both screen
:>and file I/O (Using "native" COBOL OPEN, READ, WRITE, CLOSE is a DEFINITE
:>"no-no")

:>IMS uses CALL "CBLTDLI" (or slight variations) to do screen I/O and IMS
:>database access.

IMS/DC does not do screen I/O. The Message Processing Program (MPP) is invoked
with all the data needed to perform the instruction (such invocation may have
been a while before) and then returns results (which, again, may not be
displayed for a while).

:>                  It is allowable (depending on some technical situations)
:>to use OPEN, READ, WRITE, CLOSE, etc *and* EXEC SQL in an IMS program.

As allowed in CICS - though you would probably get your fingers broken for
doing it (in an MPP).

Batch programs would be allowed to do such functions.

:>Both CICS and IMS use "native COBOL" syntax for all "business logic" -
:>everything except screen and "file" I/O.  You shouldn't need any
:>"re-training" for this part.
```

There are some brilliant IMS experts on the IMS-L list.

As others have pointed out, IMS has both a data management and transaction
management component.  <IMS bigot> IMS TM, while not as popular as CICS, is
actually a superior transaction manager.  </IMS bigot>.   :-)


"Tom G" <tjguerra@attbi.com> wrote in message
news:6dc3887b.0211171255.37695f42@posting.google.com...
> tjguerra@attbi.com (Tom G) wrote in message
news:<6dc3887b.0211060746.7ca60fe1@posting.google.com>...
> > "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com> wrote in message
news:<aq9vuj$qo2$1@slb0.atl.mindspring.net>...
> > > I slightly disagree with Charles' summary.
> > >
> > >  CICS (which the original poster knows) is ONLY a "transaction
management
> > > system".  It relies on various "file" systems (including VSAM, DB2 or
even
> > > IMS) for "data storage".
> > >
> > > IMS includes two parts  - one is a hierarchical database system (which
can
> > > be accessed from batch, from CICS, *or* from an IMS transaction
system).  In
> > > addition, IMS provides its own  "transaction system"
> > >
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> > >    EXEC CICS  (and/or EXEC DLI and/or EXEC SQL) statements to do both
screen
> > > and file I/O (Using "native" COBOL OPEN, READ, WRITE, CLOSE is a
DEFINITE
> > > "no-no")
> > >
> > > IMS uses CALL "CBLTDLI" (or slight variations) to do screen I/O and
IMS
> > > database access.  It is allowable (depending on some technical
situations)
> > > to use OPEN, READ, WRITE, CLOSE, etc *and* EXEC SQL in an IMS program.
> > >
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
> > > > IMS is a database management system.  See the book "IMS for the
Cobol
> > > > Programmer" vol 1 and 2 at http://www.murach.com/books/mainframe.htm
> > > >
> > > > Tom G <tjguerra@attbi.com> wrote in article
> > > > <6dc3887b.0211051815.223625fc@posting.google.com>...
> > > > > I have been writing Cobol/CICS on the IBM/MVS platform for about
15
> > > > > years now. Unfortunately the downsize happened. I see many
positions
> > > > > in my area (Colorado)for IMS platform Cobol/CICS programmers. Can
> > > > > anyone explain to me in 25 words or less what is the difference in
the
> > > > > platforms or point me in the area where I can learn the
differences???
> > > > >
> > > > > Any help is appreciated.
…[15 more quoted lines elided]…

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** Robert Graham <rgraham2@nycap.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-18T17:23:55+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DD92418.9020300@nycap.rr.com>`
- **References:** `<6dc3887b.0211051815.223625fc@posting.google.com> <01c2853b$95acf520$168bf243@chottel> <aq9vuj$qo2$1@slb0.atl.mindspring.net> <6dc3887b.0211060746.7ca60fe1@posting.google.com> <6dc3887b.0211171255.37695f42@posting.google.com> <tf6C9.2697$kS3.279144@news20.bellglobal.com>`

```
Someone wrote:  Where are all the knowlegeable IMS people???

Yes, retired, downsized, etc.  How true.  I had been doing 
DB2 & CICS for so long that I'd almost forgotten about IMS, 
but given my current work situation (or lack thereof), I'd 
do IMS again.  I'm not proud (except of my code).


Leahy, Don wrote:
> There are some brilliant IMS experts on the IMS-L list.
> 
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> 


As far as transaction managers go, anything beats CICS. 
MicroFocus COBOL's screen handling comes to mind, but I've 
also used XEDIT in an IBM/VM environment to handle 
transactions, not pretty, but fairly easy.
```

>

> (snip)

Actually, there was someone who did figure out stats for
comp.software.year-2000 several times.  DocDwarf was one of the most

I just checked csy2k (where I still lurk) and there hasn't been a new
posting since November 1.  That's the only time since it's inception
that it has gone for five days without a single posting.  In early
January of 2000 I think it hit 1200 new postings per day.  I couldn't
keep up with it.

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com

- **Message-ID:** `<aqdgkj$1a8$1@panix1.panix.com>`

In article <3DCA1426.5D017F42@worldnet.att.net>,
Arnold Trembley  <arnold.trembley@worldnet.att.net> wrote:
>Pat Hall wrote:
>> 
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>frequent posters, but had a high rate of quoted material.

Ahhhhh, for the Oldene Dayse... when a poster could post postings  such as 
*ten* posters cannot post today!  As I recall - and my memory is, 
admittedly, porous - a lot of what went on there was simple questioning; 
since it was considered impossible to have 'proof' of what would happen as 
a result of the Big Rollover many postings would be concrete 
assertions/prediction about the outcome of the event... and the response 
would be for simple evidence.

As I recall - see above about the state of my memory - there was not much 
evidence to be had.

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: testing, one two three

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** Liam Devlin <LiamD@optonline.SPAMNOT.net>
- **Date:** 2002-11-09T08:32:21+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DCCC7E0.5060502@optonline.SPAMNOT.net>`
- **References:** `<3DC94112.3060005@Sympatico.ca> <3DC95331.2030104@Sympatico.ca> <3DC9791C.1E0061FF@certcoinc.com> <3DCA1426.5D017F42@worldnet.att.net> <aqdgkj$1a8$1@panix1.panix.com>`

```
docdwarf@panix.com wrote:
> In article <3DCA1426.5D017F42@worldnet.att.net>,
> Arnold Trembley  <arnold.trembley@worldnet.att.net> wrote:
…[22 more quoted lines elided]…
> evidence to be had.

You have that right. The NG got kidnapped by a small group of doomsayers 
who insisted that the rollover was going to be the end of civilization 
as we had come to know it. When I questioned someone who said it was 
going to be worse than the Bubonic Plague, countless people jumped down 
my throat, saying I was stupid, naive, short-sighted, the product of an 
unnatural act, etc. I followed the group for a while; but gave it up 
because I had other things to do, e.g., rewrite the application's date 
routine to make it y2k compliant:-)

I checked back into c.s.y2k after 1/1/2000 and was amazed to see that 
the same arguments were still raging, usually "everything was going to 
crap out soon".
```

…[10 more quoted lines elided]…

Most likely... but there's always deja... errrrr, google:

<http://groups.google.com/groups?selm=AF57408D86ACA4B9.D32907705E6F1BA9.710DB33E14970799%40lp.airnews.net&output=gplain>

... posted 3 Jan 00 and it includes the classic:

--begin quoted text:

The history of Y2K remediation and early failures has been nothing but
one lie after another.  Not one single organization has ever told the
truth, either about the state of their remediation or the facts of
their actual failures.

[snip]

The proof of this is in the _lack_ of reports of major failures since
the rollover.

[snip]

To say there
have been _no_ significant failures is clearly a lie and the victims
are the pollys, not the doomers.

--end quoted text

So... the logical conclusion - since the days of 'reporting' are long over 
and the effects have become manifestly clear - is that the fact that there 
were no widespread effect from failures means that an assertion of no 
widespread failures is 'clearly a lie'.

So... the fact that it isn't there clearly demonstrates that it *must* be 
there... you just cannot *see*, you poor, deluded, poopie-head!

DD
```

>"Amazing Grace", "was blind but now I see".  :-)

You think it calls for Rear Admiral Hopper to play a tune on the bagpipes?  
Well, that'd be Amazing, Grace!  I think it calls for a gentle smile over 
days gone by and the realisation that - as the Germans say - plus ca 
change, plus c'est la meme chose... just 'tother day I was talking with a 
fellow who claimed that the war-sabers rattling in the Middle East bode no 
good and the only thing to do was to leave the cities and stockpile 
necessities...

... and when I asked if he had had similar suggestions about Y2K he just 
looked daggers at me and muttered something about 'always good to be 
Prepared'.

DD
```

Well, at the time (as Y2K approached) I was commuting over 
very icy country roads to get to work, and I was hoping 
everything would collapse so I could stay home an play in 
the snow with the kids...no such luck.  However, I still 
ride into town on Sunday for church (by auto, not buggy), 
but I would starve in an agricultural situation, the only 
thing that grows well in my garden is weeds, and they are 
not the kind anyone smokes.

Alistair Maclean wrote:

> ... Where are the Cobol jobs now?
> 
> 
Now that IS a good question.  There are none where I live.  Why is it
that they allow JAVA programmers to telecommute, but they want COBOL 
programmers on site and under their noses?  I've got 20 years experience 
in COBOL and they let the "flavor of the week" work from a laptop on the 
beach in Florida.  Bummer.  (I hope I don't turn into a bitter old COBOL 
programmer ;(

I am having to hunt for work far and wide (although an assignment on 
site in Scotland might draw me out of my house in western Mass. USA.  It 
is tough to move about with a family all entrenched in local activities.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: testing, one two three

_(thread depth: 9)_

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@nospam.enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-11-11T23:40:12
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3dd17fae_5@Usenet.com>`
- **References:** `<3DC94112.3060005@Sympatico.ca> <3DC95331.2030104@Sympatico.ca> <3DC9791C.1E0061FF@certcoinc.com> <3DCA1426.5D017F42@worldnet.att.net> <aqdgkj$1a8$1@panix1.panix.com> <3DCCC7E0.5060502@optonline.SPAMNOT.net> <pZKZTIAdnXz9EwAm@ld50macca.demon.co.uk> <3DCFE091.5070400@nycap.rr.com>`

```
Alistair,

check out http://www.jobserve.co.uk

Run a search for COBOL... I looked at it today just out of interest and
there were 60 jobs there. Many of these are the same job being posted by
different Agencies, but there are still realistically 20 or so jobs. Some of
them are in Ireland; but they want people for the East Coast US. Rates are
not great, but work could be long term. It made me smile to see one job in
Finland where they want 30 people for 4 years, but they MUST speak Finnish!

If you monitor this site regularly, you might well get something.

Best of Luck,

Pete.

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: c.s.y2k (was: testing, one two three)

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** "Joel C. Ewing" <jcewing@acm.org>
- **Date:** 2002-11-12T03:28:51+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DD075BF.9040903@acm.org>`
- **References:** `<3DC94112.3060005@Sympatico.ca> <3DC95331.2030104@Sympatico.ca> <3DC9791C.1E0061FF@certcoinc.com> <3DCA1426.5D017F42@worldnet.att.net> <aqdgkj$1a8$1@panix1.panix.com> <3DCCC7E0.5060502@optonline.SPAMNOT.net>`

```


Liam Devlin wrote:
...
> You have that right. The NG got kidnapped by a small group of doomsayers 
> who insisted that the rollover was going to be the end of civilization 
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
> crap out soon".

I was one of 476 voting to start c.s.y2k in October 1996 as a clearing house
  for serious discussion of y2k solutions by people who were primarily 
interested in identifying and fixing
  potential software problems.  By 1999, perhaps earlier, there was 
little  serious discussion left:  Anything that needed to be said had 
been said at least 100 times; any one actively engaged in code 
remediation knew what to do and was either finished or actively engaged 
in a well defined process; and those that now understood the problem and 
solutions also regarded anyone just starting to get interested in late 
1999 as not part of the solution and probably clueless.

The group was not so much "kidnapped" as "abandoned" by mid 1999, as no 
longer relevant to the solution.  The doomsayers that filled the void 
appeared to be largely non technical types with no direct experience in 
dealing with the problem, feeding off each other's worst case scenario 
unimpeded by logic or experience.  You can't discuss rationally with 
such people because they aren't driven by facts or logic.  The last 
posting I saw in c.s.y2k that was useful enough to bother saving was in 
May 1999.
```

## Thread: problem microfocus cobol — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### problem microfocus cobol

- **From:** "papl" <pascal.pleis@ctrceal.caisse-epargne.fr>
- **Date:** 2002-11-06T16:21:13+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqbc1n$7ne$1@s1.read.news.oleane.net>`

```
I have this message when execute a microfocus cobol
Due to misuse, a reboot is required ?

help me !!
```

#### ↳ Re: problem microfocus cobol

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-11-07T07:56:57+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DCA1CC9.A52D6B1C@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<aqbc1n$7ne$1@s1.read.news.oleane.net>`

```
papl wrote:
> 
> I have this message when execute a microfocus cobol
> Due to misuse, a reboot is required ?
> 
> help me !!

Response version 1:

On the front of the computer case (the metal box thing under the desk,
or follow the keyboard cable) you will find two buttons.  The larger one
should be marked '0|1' or similar, the smaller one 'Reset'.  Press the
smaller one.

If, upon trying this program again several times, you get the same
message each time, then press the larger button once only.  You will
then not get this message again.


Reponse version 2:

This is not a MicroFocus message that I have seen before, but that may
only be because I never misused it that way.

If this is a program that you wrote yourself then you should be able to
animate it, or debug it, to locate where it is occuring.  

If it is not a program that you wrote then it may be a message written
by the programmer for reasons that only he would know.
```

## Thread: Problem running Cobol program on OpenVMS via Telnet — 2 msgs

### Problem running Cobol program on OpenVMS via Telnet

- **From:** Joseph Norris <jozefn@newbolt.sonic.net>
- **Date:** 2002-11-07T17:01:24+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Pine.LNX.4.40.0211070900310.1565-100000@newbolt.sonic.net>`

```


Here is my problem.

I am using Net::Telnet to telnet into a OpenVms box and run a cobol
program. I have set the script up as below. The Cobol program at this
point is a test program that just does two displays.

The problem is that the displays from the Cobol program do not show
up and so my waitfor's do not make it and the script dies.

The last entry in the log file just shows the name of the Cobol program
but it appears that nothing is running and then the perl script
proceeds to the next statement and logs off.

Is this a buffering problem on the part of the Cobol program?
Is the Cobol program in this OS understanding that it is not really
talking to a telnet session but a pro-grammatically invoked telnet session?

I can telnet from my box to the vms box and run the program on line
with no problem.


I know Cobol and perl but mainly on Hp3000 and Unix boxes - very
new to OpenVms.

Thanks to all who can give me any direction.
Any and all help would be appreciated - need to get this to run.


use Net::Telnet ();
 $username = 'dpsub_1';
 $passwd = 'some_pass';

 $filename = 'telnet.log';

 unlink $filename;

 $t = new Net::Telnet (Timeout => 60);
    $fh = $t->dump_log;
    $fh = $t->dump_log($fh);
    $fh = $t->dump_log($filename);
print "dump set\n";
    $t->open("machine.ip.kept.secret");
print "at open\n";
    $t->login(Name=>$username, Password=>$passwd, Prompt=>'/\$/');
print "at dir cmd\n";
    $t->print("run testcob.exe");
        @lines = $t->getlines;
    $t->print("logout");
    $t->close;

my $cnt = 0;
for (@lines){
        $cnt++;
        print "$cnt: $_\n";
}
exit(0);

Joseph Norris (Perl - what else is there?/Linux/CGI/Mysql)
print @c=map chr $_+100,(6,17,15,16,-68,-3,10,11,16,4,1,14,-68,12,1,14,8,
-68,4,-3,-1,7,1,14,-68,-26,11,15,1,12,4,-68,-22,11,14,14,5,15,-90);
```

#### ↳ Re: Problem running Cobol program on OpenVMS via Telnet

- **From:** Alain Chappuis <alain.chappuis@medecine.unige.ch>
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T08:51:36+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DCB6D08.3090009@medecine.unige.ch>`
- **References:** `<Pine.LNX.4.40.0211070900310.1565-100000@newbolt.sonic.net>`

```
Joseph Norris wrote:
> 
> Here is my problem.
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
> up and so my waitfor's do not make it and the script dies.

In the VMS box please add the following command line juste
before the command run:

$       DEFINE  /USER SYS$INPUT SYS$COMMAND

Here is an example:

$       IF      P3 .EQS. "TEST" THEN DEFINE COB$SWITCHES "2"
$       PGM   = "$EXE:''P1'.EXE"
$       DEFINE  /USER SYS$INPUT SYS$COMMAND
$       PGM     "''P2'"

Note here P1 is the name of executable program where $EXE: is the directory
P2 is the parameter for the program running

You need to use the CLI command parameter.

I hope in this help.

Alain
```

wrote:

:>>:>information?

:>Out of curiousity, Binyamin, what the heck is the JESYSMSG log?

There are three system output files related to each job - JESMSG, JESJCL &
SYSMSG.

wrote:

>Hi, I want to write some messages into JESYSMSG log from my COBOL
>program. Can anyone help me how to do that / tell me where can get the
>information?
>
>Thanks.

Jay Moseley, CCP
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Writing messages into JESYSMSG log

- **From:** "Joel C. Ewing" <jcewing@acm.org>
- **Date:** 2002-11-10T20:27:46+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DCEC18A.7040803@acm.org>`
- **References:** `<3dcac523$1_2@news.onlynews.com> <pdjlsuo5418dp02g65v7p830bq7l3b7mc6@4ax.com>`

```


Jay Moseley wrote:
> On 7 Nov 2002 14:55:15 -0500, vigneshk_au <member@mainframeforum.com>
> wrote:
>>Hi, I want to write some messages into JESYSMSG log from my COBOL
>>program. Can anyone help me how to do that / tell me where can get the
>>information?
...
> 
> Isn't DISPLAY 'message text' UPON CONSOLE what you are looking for?
> Jay Moseley, CCP

But keep in mind these messages don't just go to the job JESYSMSG log 
but also to the system log and probably also get displayed on MVS 
consoles.  Don't use this for writing 10,000 lines of debugging output 
(I've seen it done), or you will get Operators and others highly ticked 
off, and possibly even cause other applications to grind to a halt if 
consoles can't keep up and you cause a [hopefully temporary] exhaustion 
of console message buffers.  At our installation, a job that floods the 
console log with messages will be canceled.

Some installations also have a standard that says console messages 
should only be written by a program when they convey information useful 
to Operators for proper execution of the job.  There is already much 
clutter on the system log without adding something that doesn't really 
belong there.  If it's really information that is only relevant to the 
application and not to the Operators, an ordinary SYSOUT data set rather 
than the console would be a better choice.
```

#### ↳ Re: Writing messages into JESYSMSG log

- **From:** Alistair Maclean <alistair@ld50macca.demon.co.uk>
- **Date:** 2002-11-09T21:14:40+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<kJdanNAArXz9EwAZ@ld50macca.demon.co.uk>`
- **References:** `<3dcac523$1_2@news.onlynews.com>`

```
If you are doing this in a commercial environment then you might find
that your companies' standards do not allow messages to the log.

In article <3dcac523$1_2@news.onlynews.com>, vigneshk_au
<member@mainframeforum.com> writes
>Hi, I want to write some messages into JESYSMSG log from my COBOL
>program. Can anyone help me how to do that / tell me where can get the
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
>www.MainFrameForum.com - USENET Gateway
```

## Thread: Relativity Database Designer Error — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### Relativity Database Designer Error

- **From:** cobolnotdead@hotmail.com (Kate Parker)
- **Date:** 2002-11-07T13:00:01-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<49973e5b.0211071300.187efe25@posting.google.com>`

```
Hello,

I'm getting the following errors when I attempt to run Liant's
Relativity Database Designer version 2.20 on Win 2000 Professional and
Win 98:

"Unable to access Code Page information:  Cannot find ListCharsets in
setup DLL (C:\RELDBMS\RELDBM32.DLL)"

and then "Runtime error '9' subscript out of range" when I attempt to
add a RMCobol object file (version 6) to the database.

Relativity's documentation does not mention this specific error
message.
Has anyone else run into this error?

Thanks,
Kate Parker
```

#### ↳ Re: Relativity Database Designer Error

- **From:** "Tom Morrison" <t.morrison@liant.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-07T21:35:52+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Y0By9.1197$4F1.711226423@newssvr11.news.prodigy.com>`
- **References:** `<49973e5b.0211071300.187efe25@posting.google.com>`

```
"Kate Parker" <cobolnotdead@hotmail.com> wrote in message
news:49973e5b.0211071300.187efe25@posting.google.com...
> Hello,
>
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> setup DLL (C:\RELDBMS\RELDBM32.DLL)"
[snip]

Kate,

Version 2.20 is *very* old (in software years, at least).  Current version
is version 4.xx.  This problem was fixed years ago.

Please contact Liant support at rm_support@liant.com for further information
```

<a id="t-8f9957a619"></a>
## Thread: Nigerian email scam — 17 msgs

### Nigerian email scam

- **From:** "Charles Hottel" <chottel@cpcug.org>
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T00:39:32+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<01c286bf$48e5f220$9293f243@chottel>`

```
I have be receiving a lot of these lately. Todays paper had some advice:

From the Washington Post, Thursday, Nov. 7, 2002.
Business page E3: "The Color Of Money" by Michelle Singletary

Q: My company received a Nigerian scam letter. ... What should we do?

A: ... 

The U.S. Secret Service says to forward emails that appear to be
"419" cons (named after the section of the Nigerian penal code 
that addresses fraud schemes) to 419.fcd@usss.treas.gov. The Secret
Service is building a database of the different versions of the scam.
It also shares the information with other law enforcement agencies.

If you have received a 419 con letter via regular mail or fax, but have not
lost any money, you can fax a copy of the pitch to 202-406-5031. If you
have been victimized by one of these schemes, you should forward any
written documentation to the Secret Service, Financial Crimes Division, 950
H St., NW, Washington, DC 20223 or telephone the agency at 202-406-5850.

A word to the wise: Don't do what I did. I was intrigued by what would
happen if I responded to one of these emails. I had no intention of
participating in the dishonest deal, but as a reporter I was curious.  An
onslaught of emails came back directing me to .... Months later, I'm still
paying for my curisoity. Now I receive triple the number of emails from
these crooks.
```

Charles Hottel

I will tell you, what will happens  if you go to end.
I received this  letter too, and I responded all of them, of course  with a
wrong name.

He ( the person behind the scam) will ask you to give him a Account number
outside your country ( as usual, we dont have)  he drive you to open an
account in a netherland bank ( this bank doenst Exist) , them after this
he call you (using all the information you gave  when  you opened an account)
and tell you that there are 2 bags(lugage) with the Money  in a Safe company
in holland and for  you get this money you must pay  10.000 US$ cash to this
company.

he meets  you in holland, and as only you and him knows that you are
bringing this money, when you arrive, they  cath you.

think, you are out of you  hometown, you know nobody, will you call the police
?
that is the end.

there are some variations  around  this

ps.  all the companies envolved doesnt exists, only in the Internet

C lages
Rio de Janeiro

- **From:** "Ryan Ware" <ryanware1@hotmail.com>

Never thought of forwarding them to the Secret Service.  I just put a filter
on the mail server to dump them with the rest of the spam.  There was just a
news story last week on NPR and somewhere on the web about how this scam is
helping to bring internet connectivity to Africa.  I don't see how people
fall for this, but I guess they do, or we wouldn't see so many of them.

Ryan

LOL

I just got one from "Abdul Rasaq Esq." in Nigeria. It's fun to watch 
these things evolve, this guy includes a link so the receiver of the 
scam spam can verify that it's not a hoax:-)

   ----------------------------------------------------------

Dear Sir,

First I must apologise for intruding on your privacy, it was somewhat 
unaviodable. I came across your name through the internet and decided to 
contact you with a pressing problem that has the potential of great 
benefit to you.

(snipped)

I look forward to your prompt and positive response.

Regards,
Abdul Rasaq Esq.


N.B. For you to believe this is not a hoax you can browse through this 
web site.

http://www.transnationale.org/anglais/sources/tiersmonde/dirigeants__abacha.htm

    ---------------------------------------------------------------------

The link seems to be in French, I thought Nigeria was an English 
speaking country & former English colony?

<a id="t-d9878c46df"></a>
## Thread: Need opinion on COBOL-VSAM-DB2 conversion — 15 msgs

_Topics: [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases) · [`VSAM, files, sorting`](topics.md#files)_

### Need opinion on COBOL-VSAM-DB2 conversion

- **From:** scjpmba@yahoo.com (S Jepson)
- **Date:** 2002-11-07T19:38:49-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2c37e529.0211071938.4727113f@posting.google.com>`

```
Hello Everyone!

I am a mainframe newbie trying to analyze a project that will involve
enhancing a system to support an acquired company's data.

Any help is greatly appreciated! 

i.e. Company A acquired Company B and will dispense with Company B's
accounting system.

Company A's accounting system (COBOL-VSAM-MVS) will be enhanced to
support Company B operations as well (for the near future, they will
function as separate entities).

The path of least resistance appears to be to add an additional key
(COMPANY_CD)to the VSAM files used by this system, prepopulate all
existing data with COMPANY_CD = A and create new data with COMPANY_CD
= A or B as required.

I am trying to estimate the effort of the changes required to the
online CICS programs to accomodate such a change.

1. Are there any issues that I should be aware of with this approach?

2. Is there a better (recommended) approach to accomplish the same
objective? This must be a relatively common scenario...

3. Is it better instead to leave the core programs as they are and
write control/command programs to call specific programs with company
code specified? The objective here is to leave the complicated
business logic in the core programs untouched...

4. Are there issues specific to VSAM that would impact such an
enhancement?

5. Are there issues specific to CICS browse programs that would impact
such an enhancement?

I have a bunch of non-mainframe systems experience, but my mainframe
experience is limited to some training 10 years ago - so I apologize
of some of these question are very basic - and thanks in advance to
whoever read so far and wants to reply!!
```

#### ↳ Re: Need opinion on COBOL-VSAM-DB2 conversion

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T00:49:35-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqfmqd$lsk$1@slb3.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<2c37e529.0211071938.4727113f@posting.google.com>`

```
Does your shop have DB2 and do your programmers "know" EXEC SQL?  (Your
note's subject includes DB2 - but your note never mentions it.) If you have
the software and skills, I would think that this would be an "excellent"
time to upgrade from VSAM to SQL.  Although it would take more time (to
upgrade the applications) it would provide a "better" environment for the
future.

Converting VSAM files  to SQL databases is a relatively easy task (from a
technical point of view).  However, getting a "well-normalized" database
takes more planning and development.
```

#### ↳ Re: Need opinion on COBOL-VSAM-DB2 conversion

- **From:** Binyamin Dissen <postingid@dissensoftware.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T12:02:04+02:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<qk2nsu4rteqka2aecntvuta6e9anndebs8@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<2c37e529.0211071938.4727113f@posting.google.com>`

```
On 7 Nov 2002 19:38:49 -0800 scjpmba@yahoo.com (S Jepson) wrote:

:>I am a mainframe newbie trying to analyze a project that will involve
:>enhancing a system to support an acquired company's data.

:>Any help is greatly appreciated! 

:>i.e. Company A acquired Company B and will dispense with Company B's
:>accounting system.

:>Company A's accounting system (COBOL-VSAM-MVS) will be enhanced to
:>support Company B operations as well (for the near future, they will
:>function as separate entities).

:>The path of least resistance appears to be to add an additional key
:>(COMPANY_CD)to the VSAM files used by this system, prepopulate all
:>existing data with COMPANY_CD = A and create new data with COMPANY_CD
:>= A or B as required.

I would suggest going to a database system.

:>I am trying to estimate the effort of the changes required to the
:>online CICS programs to accomodate such a change.

To specifically online CICS programs? An additional field on the screen.

:>1. Are there any issues that I should be aware of with this approach?

Look at the forest, not the trees. I am sure there are batch programs as well.

Do you have spare space in the record to include this additional value? Then
you are in some luck.

:>2. Is there a better (recommended) approach to accomplish the same
:>objective? This must be a relatively common scenario...

Convert to a database.

:>3. Is it better instead to leave the core programs as they are and
:>write control/command programs to call specific programs with company
:>code specified? The objective here is to leave the complicated
:>business logic in the core programs untouched...

How would you know which business logic to apply?

:>4. Are there issues specific to VSAM that would impact such an
:>enhancement?

Not really.

:>5. Are there issues specific to CICS browse programs that would impact
:>such an enhancement?

Different record layouts.

:>I have a bunch of non-mainframe systems experience, but my mainframe
:>experience is limited to some training 10 years ago - so I apologize
:>of some of these question are very basic - and thanks in advance to
:>whoever read so far and wants to reply!!

How would you do this on a non-mainframe?
```

#### ↳ Re: Need opinion on COBOL-VSAM-DB2 conversion

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T06:02:19-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqg5jr$f01$1@panix1.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<2c37e529.0211071938.4727113f@posting.google.com>`

```
In article <2c37e529.0211071938.4727113f@posting.google.com>,
S Jepson <scjpmba@yahoo.com> wrote:
>Hello Everyone!
>
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>accounting system.

With all due respect... this is a tasl which should not be assigned to a 
newbie; you should be working as a team member under the direction of a 
more senior programmer.

>
>Company A's accounting system (COBOL-VSAM-MVS) will be enhanced to
>support Company B operations as well (for the near future, they will
>function as separate entities).

This is one indication why a newbie should not be in a primary role... 
for a period after acquisition (at least one full quarter, preferably two 
full years) both systems should be run in parallel so that Senior 
Management can find out which one is (with 'unbiased' personnell to review 
the numbers) the most efficient.  *After* these data are gathered the 
decision can be made which system to use... and a newbie might not be the 
best person to prepare such a cost/benefit analysis (nor be a person with 
sufficient corporate 'weight' to face down a brash 'but *we* acquired, 
*our* systems *must* prevail!' with a cool 'they are all 'our' systems 
now, using this one over the other will cost us US$n/year... if you want 
that to come out of your department's budget then fine, let's move on!')

>
>The path of least resistance appears to be to add an additional key
>(COMPANY_CD)to the VSAM files used by this system, prepopulate all
>existing data with COMPANY_CD = A and create new data with COMPANY_CD
>= A or B as required.

The path of least resistance is to run in parallel (for at least a 
quarter, preferably a couple of years) and see which is cheapest.  The 
solution you present here is an 'all ya gotta do is...' and those 
solutions are almost invariably the most costly.

(*Never* trust a solution which is presented prefaced with 'All ya gotta 
do is...')

>
>I am trying to estimate the effort of the changes required to the
>online CICS programs to accomodate such a change.

The ability to estimate such things is, most frequently, one which comes 
with training and experience.

>
>1. Are there any issues that I should be aware of with this approach?

First question: what are the transaction volumes associated with each 
company's system?

Second question: How do these volumes break down in batch and online 
components?

Bring back the answers to these and how they are answered will help direct 
future efforts.

>
>2. Is there a better (recommended) approach to accomplish the same
>objective? This must be a relatively common scenario...

Yes, it is, and the relatively common response is not to put newbies in 
charge.

>
>3. Is it better instead to leave the core programs as they are and
>write control/command programs to call specific programs with company
>code specified? The objective here is to leave the complicated
>business logic in the core programs untouched...

No, the objective is to Make Money; this is done by seeing which system is 
the more efficient.

>
>4. Are there issues specific to VSAM that would impact such an
>enhancement?

This cannot be answered with the amount of information at hand.

>
>5. Are there issues specific to CICS browse programs that would impact
>such an enhancement?

See response to 4. above.


>
>I have a bunch of non-mainframe systems experience, but my mainframe
>experience is limited to some training 10 years ago - so I apologize
>of some of these question are very basic - and thanks in advance to
>whoever read so far and wants to reply!!

With all due respect, once again: get a team of people and a leader with 
more experience.

DD
```

>accounting system.

With all due respect... this is a task which should not be assigned to a 
newbie; you should be working as a team member under the direction of a 
more senior programmer.

>
>Company A's accounting system (COBOL-VSAM-MVS) will be enhanced to
>support Company B operations as well (for the near future, they will

This is one indication why a newbie should not be in a primary role...  
for a period after acquisition (at least one full quarter, preferably two
full years) both systems should be run in parallel so that Senior
Management can find out which one is (with 'unbiased' personnell to review
the numbers) the most efficient.  *After* these data are gathered the
decision can be made which system to use... and a newbie might not be the
best person to prepare such a cost/benefit analysis... nor be a person
with sufficient corporate 'weight' to face down a brash 'but *we*
acquired, *our* systems *must* prevail!' with a cool 'they are all 'our'
systems now, using A over B will cost us US$n/year... are you volunteering 
this out of your department's budget?  Good, let's move on, shall we?'

>
>The path of least resistance appears to be to add an additional key
>(COMPANY_CD)to the VSAM files used by this system, prepopulate all

>= A or B as required.

The path of least resistance is to run in parallel (for at least a 
quarter, preferably a couple of years) and see which is cheapest.  The 
solution you present here is an 'all ya gotta do is...' and those 
solutions are almost invariably the most costly.

(*Never* trust a solution which is presented prefaced with 'All ya gotta 
do is...')

>
>I am trying to estimate the effort of the changes required to the
>online CICS programs to accomodate such a change.

The ability to estimate such things with any sort of accuracy is, most 
frequently, one which comes with training and experience; *anyone* can 
reach into an orifice and pull out a number.  Nothing much good ever came 
of 'Hey, Rocky, watch me pull a number outta my hat!'

>
>1. Are there any issues that I should be aware of with this approach?

First question: what are the transaction volumes associated with each 
company's system?

Second question: How do these volumes break down in batch and online 
components? (Batch, of course, consists of permanent and temporary 
components... but *everyone* knows that, right?)

Bring back the answers to these and how they are answered will help direct 
future efforts.

>
>2. Is there a better (recommended) approach to accomplish the same
>objective? This must be a relatively common scenario...

Yes, it is, and the relatively common response is not to put newbies in 
charge.

>
>3. Is it better instead to leave the core programs as they are and
>write control/command programs to call specific programs with company
>code specified? The objective here is to leave the complicated
>business logic in the core programs untouched...

No, the objective is to Make Money; this is usually done best by starting 
with the knowledge of which system is more efficient.

>
>4. Are there issues specific to VSAM that would impact such an
>enhancement?

This cannot be answered accurately with the amount of information at hand.

>
>5. Are there issues specific to CICS browse programs that would impact
>such an enhancement?

See response to 4. above.

>
>I have a bunch of non-mainframe systems experience, but my mainframe
>experience is limited to some training 10 years ago - so I apologize
>of some of these question are very basic - and thanks in advance to

#### ↳ Re: Need opinion on COBOL-VSAM-DB2 conversion

- **From:** dashwood@enternet.co.nz (Peter E. C. Dashwood)
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T03:24:54-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<b3638c46.0211080324.15be1999@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<2c37e529.0211071938.4727113f@posting.google.com>`

```
The addition of a key is not a bad solution.

However, in your heading for this thread you say "COBOL-VSAM-DB2
conversion"

I find no mention of DB2 in the text of your post.

As you are going to repopulate the existing VSAM data anyway, there
might be some benefit in taking this opportunity to move the data to
DB2.

The main benefit in doing this is that it is then accessible with
standard software via ODBC and you can run ad hoc queries and reports
from it with SQL, rather than having to write a COBOL program every
time you need data.

Of course, you would need to convert the existing software to access
the RDB instead of the VSAM data, but this is not such a big deal
(providing the software is layered so that the data access is
isolated...).

From the CICS point of view, it is all the same to CICS whether you
are accessing RDB or VSAM; in either case it is done through the CICS
command interface.

Pete.

scjpmba@yahoo.com (S Jepson) wrote in message news:<2c37e529.0211071938.4727113f@posting.google.com>...
> Hello Everyone!
> 
…[39 more quoted lines elided]…
> whoever read so far and wants to reply!!
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Need opinion on COBOL-VSAM-DB2 conversion

- **From:** scjpmba@yahoo.com (S Jepson)
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T08:23:09-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2c37e529.0211080823.2e56f296@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<2c37e529.0211071938.4727113f@posting.google.com> <HrOy9.5022$mN6.2019899@newssrv26.news.prodigy.com>`

```
Thanks for all your answers! Yes, I guess I deserve some of the rants
as well, but then I do the work allocated and seeks considered
opinions for areas outside my expertise.

Trying to answer all the follow up questions:

1. DB2 is part of the eventual direction of the project. The system
will be initially enhanced to add the additional key (this will ensure
support for future acquisitions as well). The second phase of the
project will be to move the system to DB2. We considered doing it all
in one shot, but senior management deems it a HUGE business risk to do
bothe enhancements at one go.

2. EXEC SQL, EXEC CICS and EXEC CBLTDLI are all part of the skill set.
Its a matter of getting the resources allocated in a situation where
everyone is stretched and having to carry a larger than usual
workload. Hence the decision to do the enhancement first and move to
DB2 later.

3. There are numerours batch programs (way more than online), some of
which are very complicated which also helps influence the decision to
approach the project in two steps - reduced risk.

4. A non-mainframe i.e. RDBMS approach to this is to modify the
existing key structure and modify all data access modules. Huge ripple
effects through out the system :(

5. The VSAM record layout does have additional space to accomodate the
new key.

6. Both systems have already run in parallel for some time. A future
acquisition has now brought about some rethinking and speeded up the
conversion effort.

6. Transaction volumes are in a 60-40 split between batch and online.
Batch includes preparing data sets to feed other backoffice systems.

7. Unfortunately all data and screen IO is not localized. The
enhancement will require changes to a large section of the programs.

Thanks for all your replies - I'll take them all, thats the way I'll
learn.

SJ.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Need opinion on COBOL-VSAM-DB2 conversion

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T12:05:21-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqgqsh$s9a$1@panix1.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<2c37e529.0211071938.4727113f@posting.google.com> <HrOy9.5022$mN6.2019899@newssrv26.news.prodigy.com> <2c37e529.0211080823.2e56f296@posting.google.com>`

```
In article <2c37e529.0211080823.2e56f296@posting.google.com>,
S Jepson <scjpmba@yahoo.com> wrote:
>Thanks for all your answers! Yes, I guess I deserve some of the rants
>as well, but then I do the work allocated and seeks considered
>opinions for areas outside my expertise.

Rants?  Been rather calm, so far... but that can change at a moment's 
notice.  My main point, however, is that it appears the work has been 
allocated to you inappropriately; one can allocate to a recent tech-school 
graduate the task of race-tuning a Maserati... and onw will, most likely, 
be disappointed in the results.

>
>Trying to answer all the follow up questions:
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>bothe enhancements at one go.

Gah... it can be argued that senior management is increasing both business 
risk and expense by the course it has chosen.  Rather than:

Convert B to A
Debug A to the point where it works
Convert A to DB2 system
Debug DB2 system to the point where it works

... it might be less of a risk to:

Analyse processes for businesses involved
Generate DB2 system to cover these
Convert A and B to DB2
Debug DB2 to the point where it works

... because the analysis would be done with the capabilities of DB2 *in 
mind* and better exploiting the DB2 to serve business needs instead of 
concentrating on reproducing an already-known VSAM system in DB2.

>
>2. EXEC SQL, EXEC CICS and EXEC CBLTDLI are all part of the skill set.
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>DB2 later.

Are the resources being streteched due to fire-fighting, routine 
maintenance or development/enhancement?  Each of these would have to be 
addressed in a different manner.

>
>3. There are numerours batch programs (way more than online), some of
>which are very complicated which also helps influence the decision to
>approach the project in two steps - reduced risk.

Possibly false economy... see 1. above.

>
>4. A non-mainframe i.e. RDBMS approach to this is to modify the
>existing key structure and modify all data access modules. Huge ripple
>effects through out the system :(

This need not be addressed, then.

>
>5. The VSAM record layout does have additional space to accomodate the
>new key.

This is nice to know.

>
>6. Both systems have already run in parallel for some time. A future
>acquisition has now brought about some rethinking and speeded up the
>conversion effort.

Act in haste, repent in leisure; if the house is not in order for the 
first acquisition then moving on to a second one might be seen as 
premature... of course, try telling that to folks who are swept up in Sexy 
Takeover Fever.

>
>6. Transaction volumes are in a 60-40 split between batch and online.
>Batch includes preparing data sets to feed other backoffice systems.

This is a ratio, not a volume... it is like saying 'Bob's height is 
15/17ths of Mary's'.  What is the transaction volume, please?

>
>7. Unfortunately all data and screen IO is not localized. The
>enhancement will require changes to a large section of the programs.

This is a good argument for designing a new DB2 system to cover both 
corporate entities.

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Need opinion on COBOL-VSAM-DB2 conversion

- **From:** "Peter E. C. Dashwood" <dashwood@nospam.enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-11-10T09:58:52
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3dce3047_7@Usenet.com>`
- **References:** `<2c37e529.0211071938.4727113f@posting.google.com> <HrOy9.5022$mN6.2019899@newssrv26.news.prodigy.com> <2c37e529.0211080823.2e56f296@posting.google.com>`

```
It looks to me as though your approach is fine.(Your attitude is certainly
positive <G>).

Doc's point about considering a DB2 implementation BEFORE converting is very
valid but it isn't ESSENTIAL. Sadly, most managers simply don't understand
the real benefits of using RDB and are quite happy to emulate an existing
VSAM  system with a Relational Database. (I have software tools that will
analyse the COBOL definition of your VSAM files and generate a Relational
equivalent in third normal form. While I accept that this is NOT the best
way to implement RDB, it is a perfectly valid STARTING point, and at least
it unlocks the data so it can be accessed by standard software tools.)

I wouldn't worry too much about the fact that you are a newbie at this.

You come across as a very sensible newbie <G>. I reckon you'll succeed OK...

Pete.




 Posted Via Usenet.com Premium Usenet Newsgroup Services
----------------------------------------------------------
    ** SPEED ** RETENTION ** COMPLETION ** ANONYMITY **
----------------------------------------------------------        
                http://www.usenet.com
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Need opinion on COBOL-VSAM-DB2 conversion

- **From:** B Berman <bberman@netbox.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-13T19:52:25+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DD2ADEC.B4D08A22@netbox.com>`
- **References:** `<2c37e529.0211071938.4727113f@posting.google.com> <HrOy9.5022$mN6.2019899@newssrv26.news.prodigy.com> <2c37e529.0211080823.2e56f296@posting.google.com>`

```
S Jepson wrote:

> 2. EXEC SQL, EXEC CICS and EXEC CBLTDLI are all part of the skill set.
>

Here's a new twist to add to the mix.  EXEC CBLTDLI is an IMS-DB call, and
tells me there is already a hierarchial database used in the system.  Has
anyone considered continuing with this database system, or has the
decision been made to move to DB2 entirely.  Many installations have been
using IMS for decades and are very satisfied with their investment.
Depends on the eventual direction of the company's IT effort.  Perhaps
they are anticipating moving to client/server architecture in the future,
and want to get started with RDMS now.

Regards-
Bob
```

## Thread: [PowerCOBOL6.1a] How to set a common COPY search path — 8 msgs

_Topics: [`Language features and syntax`](topics.md#syntax) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### [PowerCOBOL6.1a] How to set a common COPY search path

- **From:** "R. Hendriks" <R.Hendriks_1@uvt.nl>
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T11:24:39+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqg3hp$2b8$1@mailnews.kub.nl>`

```
LS,

I need a common search path to COPY members for multiple PowerCOBOL
projects. I've been searching the entire documentation and am refered from
chapter to appendix to chapter to appendix to....so I didn't find it. What I
need is that every project that I make searches for a COPY member the same
way when refered to it in code. It must at first search the dir where the
project is located (for instance d:\dev\proj25\) and if it ain't found
there, then search a common dir (d:\dev\common).

Any ideas? Gracias.

Rik Hendriks
----------------------------------
'Don't think you are, know you are.'
```

#### ↳ Re: [PowerCOBOL6.1a] How to set a common COPY search path

- **From:** "JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T09:37:43-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ZcGcnShgLYGjR1agXTWcqg@giganews.com>`
- **References:** `<aqg3hp$2b8$1@mailnews.kub.nl>`

```

"R. Hendriks" <R.Hendriks_1@uvt.nl> wrote in message
news:aqg3hp$2b8$1@mailnews.kub.nl...
> LS,
>
> I need a common search path to COPY members for multiple PowerCOBOL
> projects. I've been searching the entire documentation and am refered from
> chapter to appendix to chapter to appendix to....so I didn't find it. What
I
> need is that every project that I make searches for a COPY member the same
> way when refered to it in code. It must at first search the dir where the
> project is located (for instance d:\dev\proj25\) and if it ain't found
> there, then search a common dir (d:\dev\common).

In a project, below each "Form" appear "(Script)[COBOL Script]" lines.

On each (Script) line, right-click. Pick Properties.

Pick the "Copy Folders" tab.

Type the path to some copy book members, select "Append"
Type the path to some more copy book member, select "Append"
etc.

If you can find a way to do this with an environmental command, I'd
certainly want to hear about it.
```

- **From:** "R. Hendriks" <R.Hendriks_1@uvt.nl>
- **Date:** 2002-11-12T11:19:16+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqqkn9$aop$1@mailnews.kub.nl>`
- **References:** `<aqg3hp$2b8$1@mailnews.kub.nl> <ZcGcnShgLYGjR1agXTWcqg@giganews.com>`

```

"JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com> wrote in message
news:ZcGcnShgLYGjR1agXTWcqg@giganews.com...
>
> "R. Hendriks" <R.Hendriks_1@uvt.nl> wrote in message
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> > projects. I've been searching the entire documentation and am refered
from
> > chapter to appendix to chapter to appendix to....so I didn't find it.
What
> I
> > need is that every project that I make searches for a COPY member the
same
> > way when refered to it in code. It must at first search the dir where
the
> > project is located (for instance d:\dev\proj25\) and if it ain't found
> > there, then search a common dir (d:\dev\common).
…[13 more quoted lines elided]…
>
And so I found another way :-). The cobol97 compiler uses the COB_COBCOPY
variable. PowerCOBOL invokes this compiler after pre-compiler the
'GUI-stuff' and thus you can use this variable with regard to COPY
statements. Doesn't work for INCLUDE statements, since these are
'pre-compiled' by PowerCOBOL.

Rik
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: [PowerCOBOL6.1a] How to set a common COPY search path

- **From:** "JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-12T20:35:30-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<G-OcnTCTv8DLJ0ygXTWcrg@giganews.com>`
- **References:** `<aqg3hp$2b8$1@mailnews.kub.nl> <ZcGcnShgLYGjR1agXTWcqg@giganews.com> <aqqkn9$aop$1@mailnews.kub.nl>`

```

"R. Hendriks" <R.Hendriks_1@uvt.nl>
> >
> And so I found another way :-). The cobol97 compiler uses the COB_COBCOPY
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> 'pre-compiled' by PowerCOBOL.

DANGER WILL ROBINSON!

Do not use COPY statements in the Data Divsion; Do not use #INCLUDE in the
Procedure division. (Vice-versa is a must.)

There is/was/might be a hideous bug in (at least) the COPY statement that
generates run time errors of unimaginable diagnostic complexity. Or at least
be aware of the possibility.
```

### COBOL FAQ

- **From:** wmklein@ix.netcom.com (William M. Klein)
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T11:09:15+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol,news.answers,comp.answers
- **Message-ID:** `<computer-lang/cobol-faq_1036753703@rtfm.mit.edu>`

```
Archive-name: computer-lang/cobol-faq
Posting-Frequency: twice a month
Last-Modified: 2002/05/06
Version: 3.03
URL: http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm
Reply-To: wmklein@ix.netcom.com


                            COBOL FAQ
                  (Frequently Asked Questions)

Last-Modified: Spring 2002

The full COBOL FAQ can be viewed at:

  http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm

If you have comments, corrects, deletions, or additions, please send
private email to:

    wmklein <at> ix.netcom.com

Toward the top of the FAQ is a pointer to the appendix listing the most
recent significant updates and changes.

This FAQ answers such questions as (and addresses topics such as):

 - Where can I get a COBOL compiler? 
 - Is there a free COBOL compiler? 
 - Where can I contact... ?
 - What is happening with the draft of the next COBOL Standard and what
is in it? 
 - What about OO COBOL? 
 - Books about COBOL
 - COBOL Tools
 - What can/should I post in the COBOL newsgroups?
 - What about USAGE? COMP? Storage for data in xyz format? etc?
 - How do I get started with COBOL? Where can I get education?
Tutorials? Etc

and much, MUCH more
```

I'm new at COBOL. And I'm confused writing a piece of code that does the
following:(maximum the window for viewing) I am especially confused with
Control Breaks.

WRITE A PROGRAM TO PRODUCE A REPORT THAT WILL INCLUDE CONTROL BREAKS, AS
WELL AS FINAL TOTALS.
USE THE CM2002S FILE AND RECORD LAYOUT FROM THE PREVIOUS ASSIGNMENT.

A MINOR TOTAL BREAK WILL OCCUR WHEN THE DEPARTMENT NUMBER
CHANGES, AND A MAJOR CONTROL BREAK WILL OCCUR WHEN THE STORE
NUMBER CHANGES.  FINAL TOTALS WILL BE PRINTED AT THE END OF THE
JOB.

YOU MAY DESIGN YOUR OWN REPORT LAYOUT, HOWEVER, USE THE ATTRIBUTES
FROM THE EXAMPLE.
NOTE: ZERO SUPPRESSION, AMOUNT FIELDS AND LINE SPACING.

** NOTE **     ALL FIELDS SHOWN MUST BE INCLUDED ON YOUR REPORT
               AND ABOVE ALL, YOUR REPORT MUST BE AESTHETICALLY
               PLEASING (I.E. REPORT AND COLUMNS CENTERED, SPACING

#### ↳ Re: Code Question

- **From:** "Howard Brazee" <howard.brazee@cusys.edu>
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T17:50:56+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqgti6$514$1@peabody.colorado.edu>`
- **References:** `<MMRy9.4778$L47.762264@read2.cgocable.net>`

```

On  8-Nov-2002, "Dali J" <dali@cogeco.ca> wrote:

> I'm new at COBOL. And I'm confused writing a piece of code that does the
> following:(maximum the window for viewing) I am especially confused with
> Control Breaks.

We don't have enough detail to determine where you need help.   Please include
the code you have written, the compiler you have used, and the results you have
achieved so far.

If you haven't started coding yet, code your INPUT-OUTPUT & WORKING-STORAGE. 
Then code enough to read all records and display them.  When you get done with
this, modify your code to print the output without control breaks.   When you
get done with this include the control breaks.  If you get stuck somewhere then
ask for help and show us the alternatives you have tried.   Start now, as lots
of us won't be on this forum during the weekend.

A control break is the term used to mean - when something changes, you must do
something, such as start printing a new page, or show totals for that person, or
something different.

IF DEPARTMENT-NUMBER = OLD-DEPARTMENT-NUMBER
     CONTINUE
ELSE
     PERFORM MINOR-CONTROL-BREAK
END-IF.
```

#### ↳ Re: Code Question

- **From:** "Tom Morrison" <t.morrison@liant.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T18:34:54+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<itTy9.1406$NB6.928015606@newssvr11.news.prodigy.com>`
- **References:** `<MMRy9.4778$L47.762264@read2.cgocable.net>`

```
There is an excellent example of how to code control breaks on
www.tek-tips.com

Go to that web site and search for the:
COBOL General Discussion Forum

Once you are in the COBOL General Discussion Forum, click on the "FAQs" tab
(Frequently Asked Questions).  Topic 1d is titled "How to code control
breaks."  I recommend that this is a good starting point for understanding
this basic programming concept.  (While you are in Tek-Tips, be sure to
register.  THEN, if you find the FAQ helpful, be sure to give it a good
rating; you will find the rating question at the bottom of the FAQ.)
```

#### ↳ Re: Code Question

- **From:** monkeycat <mensa@iname.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-11T20:23:31+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DD01216.1060200@iname.com>`
- **References:** `<MMRy9.4778$L47.762264@read2.cgocable.net>`

```

I note you included records but did not include the record format; 
However, I shall assume you know it (if not, you must obtain it).  I 
also assume the file is already in store, dept order (if not, include a 
sort).
My experience is that beginning programmers (and even many experienced 
programmers) get into trouble with control breaks by not following a 
simple rule: test for the control breaks immediately after reading the 
record - it should be done before doing anything with the current 
record.  If you don't do that, it's highly probable you will wind up 
with a program that's extremely difficult to debug.
The following outline will give you the basic structure of the program.
If you are just starting out, concentrate on getting the program 
structure right and hang onto that like grim death.  The details can 
then be attacked one by one without having to think of the details and 
the structure at the same time.
I've made no attempt to code basics of how to print a line, how to open 
files, etc.  You should have been trained in those details already; but 
if you are shaky in those areas, find somebody else's print program and 
study it.  Also don't forget to check your end of page line counter each 
time you print.

Good Luck!!

001-Main-routine.
	Perform 002-Housekeep-routine.
	Perform 003-Open-files-routine.
	Perform 005-Read-record-routine until EOF-switch = "E".
	Perform 007-Final-print-routine.
	Perform 004-Close-files-routine.
	Stop Run.
002-Housekeep-routine.
	Whatever.
	Exit.
003-Open-files-routine.
	Open input and output files (incl printer).
	Exit.
004-Close-files-routine.
	Close input and output files (incl printer).
	Exit.
005-Read-record-routine.
	Read the input file at end move "E" to EOF-switch.
		Check for read errors
	If EOF-switch not = "E" perform 006-Process-record.	
	Exit.
006-Process-record.
	If store#<>saved store# and not first-rec perform 008-Major.
	If dept#<>saved dept# and not first-rec perform 009-Minor.
	Add sales in record to Minor-Accum.	
	Save dept# and store# of this record.
	Perform 010-Print-deail-line.
	Exit.
007-Final-print-routine.
	Perform 008-Major.
	Perform 011-Final-total-routine.
	Exit.
008-Major.
	Perform 009-Minor-routine.
	Print major totals for the saved store#.
	Add Major-Accum to Final-Accum
	Zero Major-Accum.
	Exit.
009-Minor-routine.
	Print minor totals for the saved dept#.
	Add Minor-Accum to Major-Accum
	Zero Minor-Accum.
	Exit.
010-Print-detail-line.
	Print the detail line from the data in the current record.
	Exit
011-Final-total-routine.
	Print final totals.
	Exit.

…[184 more quoted lines elided]…

## Thread: Cobol and Java — 6 msgs

### Cobol and Java

- **From:** mpolinsk@execpc.com (Mike Polinske)
- **Date:** 2002-11-08T22:27:55-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,comp.lang.java
- **Message-ID:** `<48b8e9fa.0211082227.5b329421@posting.google.com>`

```
I am a mainframe Cobol programmer and I just took a week-long course
in Java programming to enhance my programming skills.

The course was written for C/C++ programmers and the last chapter
covered JNI.  I was wondering if it is possible to use JNI with Cobol
programs.  If JNI is not useable with Cobol, is it possible to process
files created in Cobol programs (whether sequential or indexed) in
Java and vice versa.  I know that Java doesn't have an equivalent
class for mainframe packed decimal data, so I would just use character
data.

I'd like to be able to explore it first on my own PC since I have
Fujitsu Cobol 6.1a on my own and if it works well possibly try it out
on the mainframe at work since our system does have Java installed.

Any input would be greatly appreciated.

Mike Polinske
Milwaukee, WI
```

#### ↳ Re: Cobol and Java

- **From:** thaneh@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 2002-11-09T05:12:16-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,comp.lang.java
- **Message-ID:** `<bfdfc3e8.0211090512.2e5ebf44@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<48b8e9fa.0211082227.5b329421@posting.google.com>`

```
Depending on the edition of the 6.1a you have - you may have access to
the Java Adapter classes.  These are the classes that Fujitsu provides
for Java communication.  I don't know of anything like that on the
mainframe directly, but I do know that if you are on a recent release
of z/OS that COBOL syntax can be used to invoke Java on the mainframe.
 I know of no method to invoke COBOL from Java on the mainframe.

IBM has a whole suite of software that allows you to do just this, including record classes that
allow access to VSAM data and so forth. COBOL and Java can also call each other on the mainframe,
though I have only done that through OMVS.

Contact your IBM rep and ask for information on Java and COBOL in WebSphere. (Don't just say
WebSphere or you will find yourself buried in literature and web links.:)

-Paul

Portability of Java/COBOL applications (or even coding techniques) between
Fujitsu (on the PC) and IBM mainframes is likely to be MINIMAL.

It is possible to use JNI interfaces on the mainframe *if* you are using the
most current IBM compiler, i.e. Enterprise COBOL.  With V3.2 it is even
possible to have COBOL as the "main" part of the application.

I suggest that you look at the section
  "5.2 Communicating with Java methods"

at:
 http://publibz.boulder.ibm.com/cgi-bin/bookmgr_OS390/BOOKS/IGY3PG10/5.2
```

<a id="t-6017741825"></a>
## Thread: compatibility rules - char to numeric — 5 msgs

### compatibility rules - char to numeric

- **From:** "Torsten Reichert" <Thorsten.Reichert@dresdner-bank.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-10T18:02:05+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol,ibm.software.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqm2cs$9ti1@news-1.bank.dresdner.net>`

```
....
05 GLAETTUNGSWERT      PIC S9V999999.
05 S-GLAETTUNGSWERT    PIC X(20).
....
MOVE 0,000499 TO S-GLAETTUNGSWERT
MOVE GLAETTUNGSWERT TO S-GLAETTUNGSWERT

IGYPA3005-S "GLAETTUNGSWERT (NUMERIC NON-INTEGER)" and "S-GLAETTUNGSWERT
(ALPHANUMERIC)" did not follow the "MOVE" statement compatibility rules.
The statement was discarded.

How do I correctly assign this variable  ?

Thank you very much

P.S.: Compiler is IBM C-Set on aix
```

#### ↳ Re: compatibility rules - char to numeric

- **From:** Donald Tees <Donald_Tees@Sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-11-10T12:21:49-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol,ibm.software.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DCEBFDD.4040304@Sympatico.ca>`
- **References:** `<aqm2cs$9ti1@news-1.bank.dresdner.net>`

```
A move from non numeric to numeric will NOT convert. For the move to do 
what you want,
it would have to be correctly formated, otherwise you should get a run 
time error.  

What you want to do is done with a function:

COMPUTE  GAETTUNGSWERT = FUNCTION NUMVAL (S-GAETTUNGSWERT).


Torsten Reichert wrote:

>....
>L05 GAETTUNGSWERT      PIC S9V999999.
…[17 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

#### ↳ Re: compatibility rules - char to numeric

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-10T16:16:53-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqmlt1$fme$1@slb3.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<aqm2cs$9ti1@news-1.bank.dresdner.net>`

```
Others have responded with NUMVAL solutions, but that won't work for this
case.  This is the REVERSE of a NUMVAL situation.  The code "attempts" to
move a NUMERIC (non-integer) value to an alphanumeric item.

FIRST - why would you ever want to do this?  I can't think of any "typical"
coding cases for this, but if you want to "do it" - what you probably want
is a simple redefine, i.e.

 05 GLAETTUNGSWERT      PIC S9V999999.
 05 Alpha-GLAETTUNGSWERT      redefines GLAETTUNGSWERT
                                                 Pic   X(8).
 05 S-GLAETTUNGSWERT    PIC X(20).
 ....
 MOVE 0,000499 TO S-GLAETTUNGSWERT
 MOVE Alpha-GLAETTUNGSWERT TO S-GLAETTUNGSWERT

HOWEVER, this will treat the embedded sign as part of one of the
"characters".  MAYBE, what you want is a "display" version of the numeric
field.  In which case what you want is:


 05 GLAETTUNGSWERT      PIC S9V999999.
 05 Display-GLAETTUNGSWERT      PIC +9,999999.
 05 S-GLAETTUNGSWERT    PIC X(20).
 ....
 MOVE 0,000499 TO S-GLAETTUNGSWERT
 MOVE GLAETTUNGSWERT TO Display-GLAETTUNGSWERT

Tell us what you WANT this "move" to do, and we probably can help with
better suggestions.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Use of char to numeric in COBOL

- **From:** "Torsten Reichert" <Thorsten.Reichert@dresdner-bank.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-11T10:11:52+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqnr76$9tk2@news-1.bank.dresdner.net>`
- **References:** `<aqm2cs$9ti1@news-1.bank.dresdner.net> <aqmlt1$fme$1@slb3.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
> FIRST - why would you ever want to do this?  I can't think of any
"typical" coding cases for this, but if you want to "do it" - what you
probably want is a simple redefine.

I have to build dynamic SQL-Statements without host-variables (for technical
reasons).
Our compiler setting defines "," as decimal point., whereas informix
database is set to use "."
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Use of char to numeric in COBOL

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-11T15:59:28-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqp9au$9kj$1@slb2.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<aqm2cs$9ti1@news-1.bank.dresdner.net> <aqmlt1$fme$1@slb3.atl.mindspring.net> <aqnr76$9tk2@news-1.bank.dresdner.net>`

```
In your sample, you had a numeric field with

     Pic  S9V99999

That doesn't have *either* "," or "." as the decimal point.  When you have
   Decimal-Point is Comma
in the COBOL special-names paragraph (as I assume you do) and you want to
change "," to "." in a NUMERIC-EDITED field, all you need to do is to use
INSPECT REPLACING, e.g.

   05  Num-Ed1   Pic +9,99999.
   05  Alpha redefines Num-Ed1   Pic  x(8)
        ...
  Inspect   Alpha replacing "," by "."
        or even easier (as long as this is "fixed format input") would be
  Move "." to Alpha (3:1)


HOWEVER, once again, your original post was for a numeric, not a
numeric-edited field and that has *NO* "," or "." in it.
```

#### ↳ solution to convert numerics to strings

- **From:** "Torsten Reichert" <Thorsten.Reichert@dresdner-bank.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-11T10:16:34+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol,ibm.software.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqnrg0$9te1@news-1.bank.dresdner.net>`
- **References:** `<aqm54h$9ta1@news-1.bank.dresdner.net>`

```
I have to build dynamic SQL-Statements without host-variables (for technical
reasons).
Our compiler setting defines "," as decimal point., whereas informix
database is set to use "."

...
               05 GLAETTUNGSWERT      PIC S9V999999.
               05 S-VORKOMMA       PIC S9(1).
               05 S-NACHKOMMA      PIC 9(5).
               05 S-GLAETTUNGSWERT    PIC X(20).
...
           MOVE GLAETTUNGSWERT TO S-VORKOMMA
           COMPUTE S-NACHKOMMA = ( 10^^5 ) * FUNCTION REM ( GLAETTUNGSWERT ,
1 )
           STRING
                S-VORKOMMA
                "."
                S-NACHKOMMA
            DELIMITED SPACE
            INTO S-GLAETTUNGSWERT
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: solution to convert numerics to strings

- **From:** Frederico Fonseca <frederico_fonseca@eudoramail.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-11T18:15:53+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol,ibm.software.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<kksvsu4855s8l14949i2e91ggvmr96o6h1@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<aqm54h$9ta1@news-1.bank.dresdner.net> <aqnrg0$9te1@news-1.bank.dresdner.net>`

```
On Mon, 11 Nov 2002 10:16:34 +0100, "Torsten Reichert"
<Thorsten.Reichert@dresdner-bank.com> wrote:

>I have to build dynamic SQL-Statements without host-variables (for technical
>reasons).
Could you explain why not host-variables??.
>Our compiler setting defines "," as decimal point., whereas informix
>database is set to use "."
…[15 more quoted lines elided]…
>            INTO S-GLAETTUNGSWERT
A "better ?" approach for the above code would be


05 GLAETTUNGSWERT      PIC S9V999999.
05 Edited-variable     PIC -9,999999.

move GLAETTUNGSWERT to edited-variable.
inspect edited-variable replacing "," by "."
move edited-variable to S-GLAETTUNGSWERT.


FF
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: solution to convert numerics to strings

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-11T16:51:19-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** alt.cobol,comp.lang.cobol,ibm.software.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqpc4l$3750$1@news.boulder.ibm.com>`
- **References:** `<aqm54h$9ta1@news-1.bank.dresdner.net> <aqnrg0$9te1@news-1.bank.dresdner.net> <kksvsu4855s8l14949i2e91ggvmr96o6h1@4ax.com>`

```
Frederico (and Torsten)

 I replied to this in comp.lang.cobol - but wanted to make it clear that
doing an INSPECT / REPLACING of the numeric-edited version has a "minor"
problem.  It will leave a "." where the system expects a "," as the decimal
point.  This would either lead to incompatible data or incorrect "scaling".
What you would PROBABLY want to do is to do the INSPECT / REPLACING on an
alphanumeric redefinition of the  numeric-edited item.  Alternatively (as
this is a "fixed format" field), you could simply do a

  05 GLAETTUNGSWERT      PIC S9V999999.
  05 Edited-variable     PIC -9,999999.
  05  Alpha-variable redefines Edited-Variable  Pic x(9).
        ...
  Move  GLAETTUNGSWERT     to Edited-Variable
  Move "." to Edited-Variable (3:1)

but you MUST not then use Edited-Variable as a VALID numeric-edited field.
```

## Thread: Determining Compiler Options (OS/390) — 5 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers) · [`Mainframe, z/OS, JCL, CICS`](topics.md#mainframe)_

### Determining Compiler Options (OS/390)

- **From:** ron.davis@dva.gov.au (Ron Davis)
- **Date:** 2002-11-10T21:10:27-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<67391ead.0211102110.484c55e@posting.google.com>`

```
Hi Folks,

I need to do a bulk re-compile of a large number of COBOL II and COBOL
of OS/390 programs, all under COBOL for OS/390.  Is anybody aware of a
program which will analyse a COBOL load module and report the Compiler
Options used for each COBOL module in the run unit?  Obviously I would
like to use the same options in the re-compile as were used in the
original compilation.

Surely this need must have arisen before but I can't find any
reference to it!

Thanks for any help!

Ron Davis
ron.davis@dva.gov.au
```

- **References:** `<67391ead.0211102110.484c55e@posting.google.com>`

```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Fujitsu Install on Win2k

_(thread depth: 9)_

- **From:** Donald Tees <Donald_Tees@Sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-11-11T21:20:49-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DD08FB1.5000001@Sympatico.ca>`
- **References:** `<9oUz9.6825$8o1.1139638@news.xtra.co.nz> <3DD044AC.2090903@Sympatico.ca> <jiVz9.6846$8o1.1142231@news.xtra.co.nz> <3DD04D07.9070808@Sympatico.ca> <hyVz9.6848$8o1.1142645@news.xtra.co.nz> <3DD065D8.1080408@Sympatico.ca> <gdXz9.6889$8o1.1148214@news.xtra.co.nz> <3DD07A9B.1070804@Sympatico.ca> <O5Zz9.6951$8o1.1154356@news.xtra.co.nz>`

```
aha! I think I just figured it out.  If you installed something else, 
there is a bug in the windows installer that will bugger up the path 
statement.  Re-installing windows will do the same thing.  The original 
install will set up a path that contains spaces, and must be enclosed in 
quotes for it to work.  The bug removes the spaces, so the path is 
incorrect.  As to the specific user problem, each user can be set up for 
a different environment under windows, and always could.  I suspect that 
the bottom level autoexec.bat has been buggered up, but that the user 
specific one corrects it.

Donald

James Sugrue wrote:

> This is for a windows application. The environment settings for each 
> user are exactly the same.
…[81 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Fujitsu Install on Win2k

_(thread depth: 10)_

- **From:** "James Sugrue" <jfsugrue@orcon.net.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-11-12T15:32:08+13:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol,alt.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<oKZz9.6976$8o1.1155297@news.xtra.co.nz>`

I didn't think Win2k used the autoexec.bat any more and that it got path etc from the environment settings for each user. The paths in the environment settings are the same for each as I have said.

What is the best way to install the runtime, for use with more than one user ? (Fujitsu Power Cobol 6 on win2k)
  Donald Tees <Donald_Tees@Sympatico.ca> wrote in message news:3DD08FB1.5000001@Sympatico.ca...
  aha! I think I just figured it out.  If you installed something else, there is a bug in the windows installer that will bugger up the path statement.  Re-installing windows will do the same thing.  The original install will set up a path that contains spaces, and must be enclosed in quotes for it to work.  The bug removes the spaces, so the path is incorrect.  As to the specific user problem, each user can be set up for a different environment under windows, and always could.  I suspect that the bottom level autoexec.bat has been buggered up, but that the user specific one corrects it.

  Donald

  James Sugrue wrote:

    This is for a windows application. The environment settings for each user are exactly the same.
      Donald Tees <Donald_Tees@Sympatico.ca> wrote in message news:3DD07A9B.1070804@Sympatico.ca...
      Then it is the path statement, as I said at the beginning.  Have you actually tried a path satement to check it?

      Donald

      James Sugrue wrote:

        Nothing. Just won't load, no error message.

        Running the .exe's in the /rts dir of the run-time install package works, but that needs to be done for each user.
          Donald Tees <Donald_Tees@Sympatico.ca> wrote in message news:3DD065D8.1080408@Sympatico.ca...
          What does it do?
          Donald

          James Sugrue wrote:

            Oh Ok. No that is fine. The program works when dbl click in Explorer for the user that the runtime is installed for, but no one else, not even administrator.
              Donald Tees <Donald_Tees@Sympatico.ca> wrote in message news:3DD04D07.9070808@Sympatico.ca...
              The search path for win2k.  As in the path statement.  As set in the autoexec.bat,
              or the autoexec.dos.

              Donald


              James Sugrue wrote:

Path to what ??


Donald Tees <Donald_Tees@Sympatico.ca> wrote in message
news:3DD044AC.2090903@Sympatico.ca...
  
Take a look at the path.

Donald

You can diagnose the startup of your environment with a couple
of tools from www.sysinternals.com

FileMon will show you low-level file access -- including
success/failure of the operation.

RegMon will show yo low-level registry access.

With these two tools, you can see how far the failed attempt
gets, compared to a successful one.
```

> James Sugrue wrote:
> 
> Oh Ok. No that is fine. The program works when dbl click in Explorer
> for the user that the runtime is installed for, but no one else, not
> even administrator.

Isn't that normal behaviour for users with Win2K ?  The whole point of
having 'users' is that the data and programs can be separated from each
other - privacy and security and stuff.

You probably need to check user rights and groups.
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu Install on Win2k

- **From:** sff5ky@aol.comxxx123 (Sff5ky)
- **Date:** 2002-11-12T22:27:01+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20021112172701.08236.00001245@mb-cg.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<9oUz9.6825$8o1.1139638@news.xtra.co.nz>`

```
In article <9oUz9.6825$8o1.1139638@news.xtra.co.nz>, "James Sugrue"
<jfsugrue@orcon.net.nz> writes:

>We have installed the Fujitsu runtime on a Win 2k machine. It works OK under
>the user the runtime was installed under, but doesn't for any other users.
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>

As Win2K is more WinNt like, you may need to consider how the app was
installed.
There is a prompt in WinNT (from an administrator level installation) that asks
if 
this should be available to all users.
There is also a 'global' versus 'individual' set of environment variables.
Others have indicated a need to review the PATH in autoexec.bat, with Win2K
you may need to look at System|Properties|Environment as a possible solution.
```
