## Thread: "Marketting" Language Standards — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`COBOL standards (ANS, ISO, 74/85/2002/2014)`](topics.md#standards)_

### "Marketting" Language Standards

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-11T18:03:58-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqpghs$njp$1@slb4.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
Given recent threads on problems/issues with the development of the 2002 ISO
COBOL Standard (and the apparent lack of significant implementation plans by
many vendors), I thought those in these lists might be interested in the
following note that I just received. Needless to say previous issues with
PL/I and Fortran Standards raised their own issues.  Please process,
forward, or respond as you think "appropriate".

"From: ...
Sent: Monday, November 11, 2002 11:44 AM
To: JTC1-SC22-TAG@MAILLIST.ANSI.ORG
Subject: FW: (SC22docs.1859) SC 22 N 3524 - Establishment of Ad Hoc on Mar
keting and Education


From ANSI's ISO Team (ISOT)

-----Original Message-----
From: ...
Sent: Monday, November 11, 2002 1:46 PM
To: 'SC 22 Distribution List'
Subject: (SC22docs.1859) SC 22 N 3524 - Establishment of Ad Hoc on Marketing
and Education

ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC22
Programming languages, their environments and system software interfaces
Secretariat:  U.S.A.  (ANSI)

ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC22 N3524

TITLE:
Establishment of Ad Hoc on Marketing and Education
DATE ASSIGNED:
2002-11-11

SOURCE:
SC 22 Secretariat
BACKWARD POINTER:

DOCUMENT TYPE:
Other document (Open)
PROJECT NUMBER:
N/A

STATUS:
At the SC 22 Saariselk� Plenary, Resolution 02-21 was unanimously passed
stating the following:

JTC 1/SC 22 directs its Chair and Secretariat to organize an ad hoc to
perform a marketing and education function within SC 22-related areas.

This ad hoc should examine the standards marketplace to determine areas
where SC 22 can support the development of language standards as well as
take direct action to let other organizations know the strengths that SC 22
can bring to the table. In particular, such an ad hoc could promote the idea
that SC 22 can support such standardization through working groups and
international workshops.

Additionally, this ad hoc should look for opportunities to educate possible
submitters of standards or consumers of standards about the strengths of SC
22 in the language and language environments standardization area, and how
SC 22 can help them achieve international standardization of their products.

In preparation for this Ad Hoc, the Chair has requested that a call for
comments, recommendations, and suggestions be made to SC 22 members.  Please
respond by 2003-03-01 so that the necessary planning can be done for this ad
hoc, which is intended to be held in conjunction with the next SC 22 Plenary
in Oslo.
ACTION IDENTIFIER:
COM

DUE DATE:
2003-03-01

DISTRIBUTION:
Text
CROSS REFERENCE:
DISTRIBUTION FORM:
Open

Address reply to:
ISO/IEC JTC 1/SC22 Secretariat
Matt Deane
ANSI
25 West 43rd Street
New York, NY  10036
Telephone:  (212) 642-4992
Fax:             (212) 840-2298
Email:  mdeane@ansi.org "
```

#### ↳ Re: "Marketting" Language Standards

- **From:** dashwood@enternet.co.nz (Peter E. C. Dashwood)
- **Date:** 2002-11-13T02:03:21-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<b3638c46.0211130203.75ba88b7@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<aqpghs$njp$1@slb4.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
So could we get SC22 to absorb J4, Bill? <G>

It never ceases to amaze me the way these organizations work and the
demonstrated attitudes... It's like the LAST thing anybody wants to do
is actually DO something or achieve anything. Talk about it, set up
another meeting in some pleasant location to talk about it more
(boondoggle), look for Marketing opportunities, but whatever you do,
don't actually produce anything. I guess if people are paying for the
privelege of being on these committees then they figure that the last
thing they need to do is actually "work"...

It is no wonder that their credibility has evaporated over the last 3
years.

Pete.

## Thread: Help with accumulating — 6 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help with accumulating

- **From:** JennCordell <member@dbforums.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-12T17:43:18+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2038211.1037122998@dbforums.com>`

```

My professor handed out this assignment and I'm nearly clueless -
control break processing.  I can't figure out a way to accumulate the
department, division and company hourly rates and weekly salary
averages.  Here's what I have so far:

Procedure Division.
*>
100-Main-Module.
  Perform 150-Sort.
  Perform 200-Initialize.
  Perform 300-Process until Done.
  Perform 400-Total-Line.
  Perform 900-Close.
  Stop Run.
*>
150-Sort.
  Sort Work-File on ascending key
    Division-Key
    Department-Key
      Using Employee-Data
        Giving Sorted-Employees.
*>
200-Initialize.
  Perform 210-Open-Files.
  Perform 220-Read-Detail.
  Call "Dates" using Current-Date.
  Perform 230-Set-Values.
*>
210-Open-Files.
  Open Input Sorted-Employees
      Output Division-Report.
*>
220-Read-Detail.
  Read Sorted-Employees at end move "no" to Are-There-More-Records.
*>
230-Set-Values.
  Move Current-Date to Date-Out.
  Move Department-In to Department-Ws.
  Move Division-In to Division-Ws.
*>
300-Process.
  Evaluate True
    When Division-In not equal to Division-Ws
      Perform 310-Division-Change
      Perform 362-Division-Heading
      Perform 363-Department-Heading
    When Department-In not equal to Department-Ws
      Perform 320-Department-Change
      Perform 363-Department-Heading
  End-Evaluate.
    Perform 330-Calculate.
    Perform 340-Accumulate.
    Perform 350-Format.
    Perform 360-Write.
    Perform 220-Read-Detail.
*>
310-Division-Change.
  Perform 320-Department-Change.
  Perform 311-Accumulate-Division.
  Perform 312-Format.
  Perform 313-Write-Division.
  Perform 314-Reset-Values.
*>
311-Accumulate-Division.
 Add 1 to No-Of-Employees-Div.
 Add Hourly-Rate-In to Hourly-Rate-Div.
 Add Weekly-Salary-Ws to Weekly-Salary-Div.
*>
312-Format.
  Move Division-Ws to Division-Out.
*>
313-Write-Division.
  Add 1 to Line-Count.
*>
314-Reset-Values.
  Move Division-Out to Division-Out-Total.
*>
320-Department-Change.
  Perform 321-Accumulate-Department.
  Perform 322-Format.
  Perform 323-Write.
  Perform 324-Reset-Values.
*>
321-Accumulate-Department.
  Add 1 to No-Of-Employees-Dept.
  Add Hourly-Rate-In to Hourly-Rate-Dept.
  Add Weekly-Salary-Ws to Weekly-Salary-Dept.
*>
322-Format.
   Divide Hourly-Rate-Dept by No-Of-Employees-Dept giving
    Hourly-Rate-Dept-Avg rounded.
  Divide Weekly-Salary-Dept by No-Of-Employees-Dept giving
    Weekly-Salary-Dept-Avg rounded.
*>
323-Write.
  Add 1 to Line-Count.
  Write Report-Record from Department-Heading after advancing 1 line.
  Write Report-Record from Department-Total-Line after advancing 2
  lines.
*>
324-Reset-Values.
  Move Department-Out to Department-Out-Total.
  Move Department-In to Department-Ws.
  Move zero to Accumulators.
*>
330-Calculate.
  Multiply Hours-Worked-In by Hourly-Rate-In giving Weekly-Salary-Ws.
*>
340-Accumulate.
  Add 1 to No-Of-Employees-Co.
  Add Hourly-Rate-In to Hourly-Rate-Co.
  Add Weekly-Salary-Ws to Weekly-Salary-Co.
*>
350-Format.
  Move Division-Ws to Division-Out.
  Move Department-Ws to Department-Out.
  Move Employee-Name-In to Employee-Name-Out.
  Move Hourly-Rate-In to Hourly-Rate-Out.
  Move Weekly-Salary-Ws to Weekly-Salary-Out.
*>
360-Write.
  If Line-Count > 22 then
    Perform 361-Page-Heading
    Perform 362-Division-Heading
    Perform 363-Department-Heading
  End-If.
  Perform 364-Data.
*>
361-Page-Heading.
  Add 1 to Page-Count.
  Move Page-Count to Page-Out.
  Move zero to Line-Count.
  Write Report-Record from Report-Heading after advancing page.
  Move all "-" to Report-Record.
  Write Report-Record after advancing 1 line.
*>
362-Division-Heading.
  Add 1 to Line-Count.
  Move Division-Ws to Division-Out.
  Write Report-Record from Division-Heading after advancing 2 lines.
*>
363-Department-Heading.
  Write Report-Record from Department-Heading after advancing 1 line.
  Write Report-Record from Column-Heading after advancing 2 lines.
  Write Report-Record from Under-Line after advancing zero lines.
*>
364-Data.
  Write Report-Record from Data-Line after advancing 2 lines.
*>
400-Total-Line.
  Perform 410-Build-Total-Line.
  Perform 420-Write-Total-Line.
*>
410-Build-Total-Line.
*>
420-Write-Total-Line.
  Write Report-Record from Company-Total-Line after advancing 3 lines.
*>
900-Close.
  Close Sorted-Employees
        Division-Report.

I get the actual layout of the report, just nothing for the averages.
Any help is greatly appreciated!  Thank you!

Jennifer
```

#### ↳ Re: Help with accumulating

- **From:** "Howard Brazee" <howard.brazee@cusys.edu>
- **Date:** 2002-11-12T19:18:33+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqrk6g$o8b$1@peabody.colorado.edu>`
- **References:** `<2038211.1037122998@dbforums.com>`

```

On 12-Nov-2002, JennCordell <member@dbforums.com> wrote:

> My professor handed out this assignment and I'm nearly clueless -
> control break processing.  I can't figure out a way to accumulate the
> department, division and company hourly rates and weekly salary
> averages.  Here's what I have so far:

Not a bad start - make sure you write yourself a test plan that includes such
things as divisions changing, but departments remaining the same (two divisions
have the same department name).

I prefer paragraph names that are more clear - you have 314-Reset-Values and
324-Reset-values.  To tell the truth, I don't put these in a paragraph at all.
But figure what your teacher wants.

I think what you're missing is how to calculate the weekly salary average.   To
do this, you need some intermediate values:


add salary to department-total-salary, division-total-salary,
company-total-salary.
add 1        to department-total-count, division-total-count,
company-total-count.

On your department break, COMPUTE DEPARTMENT-AVERAGE = DEPARTMENT-TOTAL-SALARY /
DEPARTMENT-TOTAL/COUNT
and reset these totals.
```

#### ↳ Re: Help with accumulating

- **From:** JennCordell <member@dbforums.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-12T22:07:00+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2039472.1037138820@dbforums.com>`
- **References:** `<2038211.1037122998@dbforums.com>`

```

I understand what you're saying...I'm just not sure how to force the
wage rate and weekly salary into their respective places and how to get
the program to count how many employees are in the department/division.
```

#### ↳ Re: Help with accumulating

- **From:** yukonmama@aol.com (YukonMama)
- **Date:** 2002-11-13T03:02:57+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20021112220257.24187.00004418@mb-mw.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<2038211.1037122998@dbforums.com>`

```
>Subject: Help with accumulating
>From: JennCordell member@dbforums.com 
>Date: 11/12/02 12:43 PM Eastern Standard Time

>My professor handed out this assignment and I'm nearly clueless -
>control break processing.  I can't figure out a way to accumulate the
>department, division and company hourly rates and weekly salary
>averages.

You're on to a good start but you're missing one vital piece of processing -
detail process, that is the processing of each individual record.

Right now your program only accumulates on a control break, which means you are
only accumulating the record that causes the break and not those inbetween.

The cycle goes something like this:
(note - this is my personal preference - there are those that do it a bit
differently)

1)  Prime read
     a)  set up control break holding fields

2)  Check for break
      a)  yes - do break
      b)  no - accumulate necessary fields to accumulator of lowest control
break
3)   Read another record

You do have the 'rolling totals' concept coded where the higher control break
calls the lower one.  In the lower control break the totals are rolled to the
next higher so at Department break the department totals are added to Division
totals and are then cleared.  At the Division break those are added to the next
higher and so on.

If the Company total is the final total that is done at end of file.

Eileen
```

#### ↳ Re: Help with accumulating

- **From:** "monkeycat" <mensa@iname.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-20T03:27:24+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<wiDC9.86318$hR3.68928@news2.central.cox.net>`
- **References:** `<2038211.1037122998@dbforums.com>`

```
I won't comment suggest actual changes to your coding - it's more important
that you have the right model in your mind.
.
Keep the following thought in mind - violating it is the most common logic
mistake of beginning programmers:
Once you have determined there is a control break, the control break
routines themselves should not do anything with the current record.  Control
break routines are dealing with totals of records you have ALREADY read.

Here's your safest sequence:

Check for control breaks and do them.
Process the current record, adding its numbers to the LOWEST control break
accumulators
Read the next record
.
.
In the LOWEST control break routine, before you zero out the LOWEST
accumulators, add them to the accumulators of the next higher control break
In the next highest control break, do the same for its next higher control
break. Etc. Etc.
.
.
.
Don't forget that end of file ALWAYS implies a control break of the highest
order.

Good Luck!

"JennCordell" <member@dbforums.com> wrote in message
news:2038211.1037122998@dbforums.com...
>
> My professor handed out this assignment and I'm nearly clueless -
…[167 more quoted lines elided]…
> Posted via http://dbforums.com
```

- **Date:** 2002-11-20T00:02:25-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

- **From:** P Goodwin <member@dbforums.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-12T21:15:15+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2039454.1037135715@dbforums.com>`

```

We just converted to OS/390 and started getting a status code 35 when
trying to open an empty VSAM file for I-O.  With COBOL II it worked
fine.  We now have to put a dummy record in it and code around the dummy
record.   Has anyone else had this problem?
```

#### ↳ Re: OS/390 Now getting open error on VSAM

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-12T16:48:04-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqs0uc$8ka$1@slb3.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<2039454.1037135715@dbforums.com>`

```
See the discussion of "available and unavailable" VSAM files at:

 http://publibz.boulder.ibm.com/cgi-bin/bookmgr_OS390/BOOKS/igy3pg10/1.10.6

What exactly did you change?  From VS COBOL II run-time to LE? VS COBOL II
compiler to Enterprise COBOL compiler? CMPR2 compiler option to NOCMPR2?

Each/any of these COULD be the cause of your problem.  (Obviously, the "fix"
is initialize the VSAM file *or* to add the "optional" phrase)
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: OS/390 Now getting open error on VSAM

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-12T17:01:55-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<aqs35q$4op$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<2039454.1037135715@dbforums.com> <aqs0uc$8ka$1@slb3.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
I just thought that I would add that this is NOT a change in the definition
of "available VSAM" files from VS COBOL II.  For the older definition, see:

 http://publibz.boulder.ibm.com/cgi-bin/bookmgr_OS390/BOOKS/igya1101/4.1.1.2

My GUESS is that you have changed from CMPR2 to NOCMPR2 - which is BOUND to
have a variety of run-time differences.
```

## Thread: help me in ims dc — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Mainframe, z/OS, JCL, CICS`](topics.md#mainframe) · [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### help me in ims dc

- **From:** vensad@yahoo.com (prasad)
- **Date:** 2002-11-15T04:52:57-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<76c695aa.0211150452.28dda1bd@posting.google.com>`

```
i'm geetting error XC when inserting the output message to the screen
using dli-isrt so plzz help me int his..??
```

#### ↳ Re: help me in ims dc

- **From:** "Leahy, Don" <zzdlf@tdbank.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-11-18T09:08:52-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Vv6C9.2729$kS3.283587@news20.bellglobal.com>`
- **References:** `<76c695aa.0211150452.28dda1bd@posting.google.com>`

```
The first four bytes of an IMS message must be LLZZ, where LL = the length
of the message (PIC S9(04) COMP) and ZZ = binary zeros.  ZZ is reserved for
the use of IMS, and *must* contain binary zeros (LOW-VALUES) when the
message is inserted.  If ZZ contains any other values, you get an 'XC'
status when you insert the message.

Hope this helps.
"prasad" <vensad@yahoo.com> wrote in message
news:76c695aa.0211150452.28dda1bd@posting.google.com...
> i'm geetting error XC when inserting the output message to the screen
> using dli-isrt so plzz help me int his..??
```

## Thread: Data Name Qualification Problem — 30 msgs

### Data Name Qualification Problem

- **From:** SkippyPB <swiegand@NOSPAM.neo.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-15T09:37:53-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net>`

```
In all my years of coding, I 've never had to use data name
qualification.  But now the need, which was precipitated by a need to
use Move Corresponding, has arisen and I've hit on something I can't
seem to solve.

Here is a very small example of two data areas that I have defined:

03  WS-OLD-TABLE-AREA  OCCURS 31 TIMES.
      05  WS-ELEMENT-1               PIC S9(9)V99  COMP-3.
      05  WS-ELEMENT-2               PIC S9(9)        COMP-3.
      05  WS-ELEMENT-3               PIC XXX.

03  WS-NEW-TABLE AREA OCCURS 31 TIMES.
      05  WS-ELEMENT-1               PIC S9(9)V99  COMP-3.
      05  WS-ELEMENT-2               PIC S9(9)        COMP-3.
      05  WS-ELEMENT-3               PIC XXX.

Later in the program I have the following:

PERFORM VARYING WS-INDEX FROM +1 BY +1
                   UNTIL WS-INDEX GREATER THAN +31
      MOVE WS-ELEMENT-1 (WS-INDEX) OF WS-OLD-TABLE-AREA
         TO   WS-ELEMENT-1 (WS-INDEX) OF WS-NEW-TABLE-AREA

      MOVE WS-ELEMENT-2 (WS-INDEX) OF WS-OLD-TABLE-AREA
         TO   WS-ELEMENT-2 (WS-INDEX) OF WS-NEW-TABLE-AREA

      MOVE WS-ELEMENT-3 (WS-INDEX) OF WS-OLD-TABLE-AREA
         TO   WS-ELEMENT-3 (WS-INDEX) OF WS-NEW-TABLE-AREA

END-PERFORM.

When the program is compiled I get E level errors on all of the MOVE
statements which says the name is not unique, i.e., duplicate name.

Is this not allowed or have I an invalid qualification?

This is occurring on mainframe, Cobol for OS/390 & VM 2.2.1

Thanks for any help.

Regards,

          ////
         (o o)
-oOO--(_)--OOo-

Real Signs:

In a Tokyo Bar:  

SPECIAL COCKTAILS FOR THE LADIES WITH NUTS
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Remove nospam to email me.

Steve
```

#### ↳ Re: Data Name Qualification Problem

- **From:** Pat Hall <phall@certcoinc.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-15T09:03:24-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DD50CBC.D9E0E2A2@certcoinc.com>`
- **References:** `<850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net>`

```
Just a guess but you may be having trouble qualifying to a level with an
occurs attached to it.  Try qualifying up one more level and see what
happens.  Otherwise I've used qualifiers before but I've always used "in"
but "of" and "in" are supposed to be interchangeable.

PatH

> In all my years of coding, I 've never had to use data name
> qualification.  But now the need, which was precipitated by a need to
…[54 more quoted lines elided]…
> Steve
```

<swiegand@NOSPAM.neo.rr.com> wrote:

>In all my years of coding, I 've never had to use data name
>qualification.  But now the need, which was precipitated by a need to

eg.

>         TO   WS-ELEMENT-3  OF WS-NEW-TABLE-AREA(WS-INDEX)

#### ↳ Re: Data Name Qualification Problem

- **From:** Tom Phillips <member@mainframeforum.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-15T10:42:52-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3dd515fc$1_2@news.onlynews.com>`
- **References:** `<850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net>`

```
I posed this same question to IBM just a week or two ago. The problem is
with your index. Your index cannot access two different tables, even
though they have the same definition. From IBM, "An index is not an
actual data item and the name is not a data name, so it cannot be
qualified to make it unique, it must actually be unique. It is more like
a special register than anything else."

Their suggestion was to change the index name in one of the tables so
you have unique index names, or use subscripting.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Data Name Qualification Problem

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-15T12:59:31-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ar3g6n$dkh$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net> <3dd515fc$1_2@news.onlynews.com>`

```
Tom,
  Can you tell me who (where) in IBM you got this INCORRECT answer?  What
you say is true for ANSI/ISO defined COBOL.  However, IBM has a documented
extension that allows you to use an index-name for a table other than the
one where it is defined.  See:

http://publibz.boulder.ibm.com/cgi-bin/bookmgr_OS390/BOOKS/IGY3LR10/APPENDIX
1.1

and look for the statement,

    "Referencing a table with an index-name defined  for a different table"


P.S.  The actual problem (as pointed out by others) in the original question
was that the person coded:

    Var1 (index-1) of Var2
        instead of
     Var1 of Var2 (index-1)
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Data Name Qualification Problem

- **From:** "Terry Heinze" <terryheinze@prodigy.net>
- **Date:** 2002-11-16T04:30:25+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<BRjB9.912$bk6.58639583@newssvr15.news.prodigy.com>`
- **References:** `<850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net> <3dd515fc$1_2@news.onlynews.com> <ar3g6n$dkh$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
I see several people keep mentioning indexes.  These tables as defined, are
not indexed; they are subscripted.  To avoid confusion, I'd avoid using the
word index in a data name used as a subscript.
```

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ar5mno$m4$1@panix1.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net> <ar3g6n$dkh$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net> <BRjB9.912$bk6.58639583@newssvr15.news.prodigy.com> <43sB9.351$Kw6.263556@newssrv26.news.prodigy.com>`

```
In article <43sB9.351$Kw6.263556@newssrv26.news.prodigy.com>,
Michael Mattias <michael.mattias@gte.net> wrote:
>"Terry Heinze" <terryheinze@prodigy.net> wrote in message
>news:BRjB9.912$bk6.58639583@newssvr15.news.prodigy.com...
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
>it must now be treated the same as a Nigerian email ....

This is why at least one consultant/contractor/hired gun lies low... and 
will ask 'What are you using to refer to this occurrence in the table?' so 
that an unwitting native will betray the Local Linguistic Use ('Oh, that's 
just one of the usual subscripts/indexes') (*never* heard 'indices')... 
and then can check the code *just to make sure* that, as it most 
frequently is, they are, in fact, using subscripts and referring to them 
as 'indexes' (it is rare that anyone under the age of fifty will, on their 
own, code SET STANDARD-IDX UP BY ONE... or so has been found)...

... and then write code that fits into this pattern.

(On a personal note... I was trained that subscripts were SUBn - not even
WS-SUBn, where the hell else would a sane person define a subscript except
for in WORKING STORAGE? - and that indices were all -IDX or -NDX...  
except, of course, when they were being coded by old BAL-jockies who
considered letters to be valuable and hoarded stingily, doling them out
with the same kind of Grand Munificence found in the Military
Quartermasters of Old, who would weep when presented with the
properly-filled-out forms that would require them to issue a soldier a
boot-lace... and an index would be suffixed with a mere -I... ahhhh, for
the Oldene Dayse, may Their like never return, pfui!)

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Data Name Qualification Problem

_(thread depth: 6)_

- **From:** SkippyPB <swiegand@NOSPAM.neo.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-16T13:26:08-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<C91C205D934F8CDC.9C47647F3AA879BC.3E628412B78295AF@lp.airnews.net>`
- **References:** `<850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net> <ar3g6n$dkh$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net> <BRjB9.912$bk6.58639583@newssvr15.news.prodigy.com> <43sB9.351$Kw6.263556@newssrv26.news.prodigy.com> <ar5mno$m4$1@panix1.panix.com>`

```
On 16 Nov 2002 10:03:20 -0500, docdwarf@panix.com enlightened us:

>In article <43sB9.351$Kw6.263556@newssrv26.news.prodigy.com>,
>Michael Mattias <michael.mattias@gte.net> wrote:
…[36 more quoted lines elided]…
>DD

Man, so much pablum over a data name!  One could easily look at the
definition of the table and see it is not indexed and that the use of
a name like WS-INDEX was as a subscript.  No need to be confused if
one does just a little research as any good programmer should do.

On my choice of using a data name like WS-INDEX for a subscript, it
comes from a company standard that says all working storage variables
not related to a record definition or table should have a WS prefix.  

As for INDEX, in my mind an index and a subscript perform the same
thing with a different name.  I suppose in the strictest sense I
should refrain from using WS-INDEX as a subscript variable, but old
habits die hard and WS-SUB just doesn't stick in my mind the same way.
So sue me!

Regards,

          ////
         (o o)
-oOO--(_)--OOo-

In a Tokyo Bar:

~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Remove nospam to email me.

Steve

Hi Skippy,

A number of years ago I worked on a batch application on an IBM mainframe
and we actually found that using INDEX ratther than mere subscripts did not
merely "perform the same thing with a different name." It was found that the
batch ran SIGNIFICANTLY faster when using INDEX rather than ordinary
subscripts. This was quite a few years ago so my knowledge here *could* be
out of date but it's worth noting.

"SkippyPB" <swiegand@NOSPAM.neo.rr.com> wrote in message
news:C91C205D934F8CDC.9C47647F3AA879BC.3E628412B78295AF@lp.airnews.net...

defined, are

using the
> >>> word index in a data name used as a subscript.
> >>
> >>Hmm, I'll bet you, too, are a contractor/consultant who has been
tricked,
> >>learning of this evil treachery and deceit only when the compiler disses
you
> >>for being so ignorant as to use SET or SEARCH on a non-index dataname or
> >>subscripted table......
> >>
> >>Sadly, this scam - known to the FBI as the "-INDEX Ploy"  - is so
ubiquitous
> >>it must now be treated the same as a Nigerian email ....
> >

so

#### ↳ Re: Data Name Qualification Problem

- **From:** docdwarf@panix.com
- **Date:** 2002-11-15T10:51:18-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ar355m$bce$1@panix1.panix.com>`
- **References:** `<850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net>`

```
In article <850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net>,
SkippyPB  <swiegand@NOSPAM.neo.rr.com> wrote:

[snip]

>03  WS-OLD-TABLE-AREA  OCCURS 31 TIMES.
>      05  WS-ELEMENT-1               PIC S9(9)V99  COMP-3.
…[26 more quoted lines elided]…
>Is this not allowed or have I an invalid qualification?

Seems not allowed... using IGYCRCTL on an OS390 I duplicated your results.  
Dumping the OCCURS from the tables and the subscripting from the 
references generated a clean compile.  Replacing the OCCURS and *not* 
subscripting the references caused errors of 'WS-ELEMENT-1 OF 
WS-OLD-TABLE-AREA" WAS A TABLE-ITEM BUT WAS NOT SUBSCRIPTED OR INDEXED' 
for all MOVEs.  Putting hardcoded subscripts back in brought it 
full-circle back to your original error.

What to do?  Well, you could tediously rename things, making the code 
clear, yea, even unto a second-year coder... or you could...

01  WS-OLD-TABLE.                                                 
    03  WS-OLD-TABLE-AREA OCCURS 31.                              
      04  WS-OLD-TABLE-ENTRY.                                     
          05  WS-ELEMENT-1               PIC S9(9)V99  COMP-3.    
          05  WS-ELEMENT-2               PIC S9(9)        COMP-3. 
          05  WS-ELEMENT-3               PIC XXX.                 
01  WS-NEW-TABLE.                                                 
    03  WS-NEW-TABLE-AREA OCCURS 31.                              
      04  WS-NEW-TABLE-ENTRY.                                     
          05  WS-ELEMENT-1               PIC S9(9)V99  COMP-3.    
          05  WS-ELEMENT-2               PIC S9(9)        COMP-3. 
          05  WS-ELEMENT-3               PIC XXX.                 

- - - - - - - -

PERFORM                                             
 VARYING WS-INDEX                                   
  FROM +1                                           
   BY LENGTH OF WS-OLD-TABLE-ENTRY                  
    UNTIL WS-INDEX > (LENGTH OF WS-OLD-TABLE /      
                      LENGTH OF WS-OLD-TABLE-ENTRY) 
      MOVE WS-OLD-TABLE(WS-INDEX:6)                 
         TO   WS-NEW-TABLE(WS-INDEX:6)              
      MOVE WS-OLD-TABLE(WS-INDEX + 6:5)             
         TO   WS-NEW-TABLE(WS-INDEX + 6:5)          
      MOVE WS-OLD-TABLE(WS-INDEX + 11:3)            
         TO   WS-NEW-TABLE(WS-INDEX + 11:3)         
END-PERFORM.                                        

... and then say 'Gee, Ma, I'm a Programmer!'

DD
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Data Name Qualification Problem

- **From:** Frederico Fonseca <frederico_fonseca@eudoramail.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-15T20:35:17+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<o4matugksv7j6570lsev5473p4i129ggk5@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net> <3DD53E1B.2060102@Sympatico.ca> <E9EFC69093F9187A.6E5AD34207F64C6D.AD52BAE82003F546@lp.airnews.net>`

```
>
>To Fredreica:  Huh?  You said:  try var1 of var2 of var3 (index)
    ^^^^^^^^^ Frederico 
>I think that is what Donald has suggested.  Since the group names are
>unique, there should be no need to go up another level in the
>hierarchy.
It was what Donald said, (and I had not seen his answer when I sent
mine.)
I mentioned three varialbles, and then the example with your's, just
to try and make clear that the "index" (and also any reference
modification if you use it) is to be positioned after the "variable is
fully referenced" , which would be required if you had something like
the following.



03  WS-OLD-TABLE-AREA  OCCURS 31 TIMES.
    04  WS-ELEMENT-2.
      05  WS-ELEMENT-3               PIC XXX.

03  WS-NEW-TABLE AREA OCCURS 31 TIMES.
    04  WS-ELEMENT-2.
      05  WS-ELEMENT-3               PIC XXX.
where you would need to use

WS-ELEMENT-3 OF WS-ELEMENT-2 OF WS-NEW-TABLE (INDEX)


Sorry if it caused any confusion to you.

FF
```

- **From:** thaneh@softwaresimple.com (Thane Hubbell)
- **Date:** 2002-11-16T05:48:59-08:00

```
I'm not answering the question directly- but I think I have a solution...

You mentioned first that you want to use Move... Corresponding...

SO change... (read on)

SkippyPB <swiegand@NOSPAM.neo.rr.com> wrote in message news:<850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net>...
> Later in the program I have the following:
> 
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
> 

 PERFORM VARYING WS-INDEX FROM +1 BY +1
                    UNTIL WS-INDEX GREATER THAN +31
       MOVE CORRESPONDING WS-OLD-TABLE-AREA (WS-INDEX) TO 
                           WS-NEW-TABLE-AREA (WS-INDEX)
 END-PERFORM.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Data Name Qualification Problem

- **From:** SkippyPB <swiegand@NOSPAM.neo.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-16T13:37:09-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<860042F0F5E01E0B.A3AE874D1F94C847.79DC640443E0C33B@lp.airnews.net>`
- **References:** `<850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net> <bfdfc3e8.0211160548.2e21407a@posting.google.com>`

```
On 16 Nov 2002 05:48:59 -0800, thaneh@softwaresimple.com (Thane
Hubbell) enlightened us:

>I'm not answering the question directly- but I think I have a solution...
>
…[25 more quoted lines elided]…
> END-PERFORM.

First off, Donald's suggestion worked.  The correct syntax for the
MOVE statement was:

MOVE WS-ELEMENT-3 OF WS-OLD-TABLE-AREA (WS-INDEX) TO
            WS-ELEMENT-3 OF WS-NEW-TABLE-AREA (WS-INDEX).

Also, for Donald's benefit, WS-INDEX as I use it is just a
Working-Storage variable described as COMP and used as a subscript.

For Thane's suggestion, as I read the OS/390 rules for use of MOVE
CORRESPONDING, you cannot use it on a table.

The IBM doc says:

The CORRESPONDING phrase (CORR) allows ADD, SUBTRACT, and MOVE
operations to be performed on elementary data items of the same name
if the group items to which they belong are specified. 

Both identifiers following the key word CORRESPONDING must name group
items. In this discussion, these identifiers are referred to as
identifier-1 and identifier-2. 

Neither identifier-1 nor identifier-2 is described as a level 66, 77,
or 88 item, nor is either described as a USAGE IS INDEX item. Neither
identifier-1 nor identifier-2 can be reference-modified. 

The subordinate items do not include a REDEFINES, RENAMES, OCCURS, 
 X USAGE IS INDEX, USAGE IS POINTER, USAGE IS PROCEDURE-POINTER, or
USAGE  X IS OBJECT REFERENCE clause in their descriptions. 

However, having said that, the IBM doc also says:

However, identifier-1 and identifier-2 themselves can contain or be
subordinate to items containing a REDEFINES or OCCURS clause in their
descriptions. 

So, maybe you are right.  I'll need to try it as soon as the need
arises.

Thanks again for everyone's help.

Regards,

          ////
         (o o)
-oOO--(_)--OOo-

Real Signs:

In a Tokyo Bar:  

SPECIAL COCKTAILS FOR THE LADIES WITH NUTS
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

Remove nospam to email me.

Steve
```

#### ↳ Re: Data Name Qualification Problem

- **From:** "Terry Heinze" <terryheinze@prodigy.net>
- **Date:** 2002-11-16T16:23:54+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<uiuB9.10181$KO7.156@newssvr19.news.prodigy.com>`
- **References:** `<850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net>`

```
Since your intent seems to be to move all 3 fields of all 31 occurrences of
the old table to the new, the same result would be achieved by adding a
couple of group names in front of each table, then moving WS-OLD-TABLE to
WS-NEW-TABLE.  (As long as the definitions, lengths, and number of
occurrences of each table remains the same.)
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Data Name Qualification Problem

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-18T17:50:27-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<arbuci$5m4$1@slb3.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<850A7195332EB9A6.7DF50350A43C88C6.9CB31E67CB7EC0B7@lp.airnews.net> <3DD95F6B.525FA53@netbox.com> <3DD97871.8040702@nycap.rr.com>`

```
I group move is an alphanumeric move (mostly).  However, it won't "mess up"
COMP-3 (or other non-DISPLAY) fields *if* they are defined the same (same
offset from the beginning and same PICTURE/USAGE within the group).  This
(ignoring the indexing/subscripting issue) would be a good candidate for a
group move. The "normal" time to use

  MOVE CORRESPONDING

is when the elementary items are NOT defined the same in the sending and
receiving fields. (For example to change all COMP-3 and BINARY fields in the
sending group to DISPLAY in the receiving group) - or when there are OTHER
fields in one of the groups that you do NOT want moved (or when the order of
the sending/receiving elementary fields are different).

My personal guess is/was that Skippy was showing a "simple" case to
demonstrate his problem, not that his actual record layouts looked this way.
If they did/do - then a group (including the entire table) would probably be
the easiest way to do it. (Depending on the compiler's optimization, that
might also be the best performing way to do it)
```

<a id="t-ea695084d0"></a>
## Thread: Using of pointers in HP Micro-Focus COBOL — 3 msgs

### Using of pointers in HP Micro-Focus COBOL

- **From:** zinovys@yahoo.com (zis)
- **Date:** 2002-11-16T09:38:06-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<bb7db173.0211160938.1bb60892@posting.google.com>`

```
I'm interested to get your advise how to use pointers 
in HP Micro-Focus COBOL.
For example, I would like to create link lists and etc.

Thank you.
```

#### ↳ Re: Using of pointers in HP Micro-Focus COBOL

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2002-11-16T22:55:44+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<Q1AB9.464$Kw6.345395@newssrv26.news.prodigy.com>`
- **References:** `<bb7db173.0211160938.1bb60892@posting.google.com>`

```
> I'm interested to get your advise how to use pointers
> in HP Micro-Focus COBOL.
> For example, I would like to create link lists and etc.

As pointed out, you can do linked lists without using pointers.

In a more general vein, you should not pick an implementation method (e.g.,
"pointers") until you have decided what to implement (e.g., "a linked list")
AND have reviewed several choices of implementations (e.g., "pointers,"
"subscripted table"  "other random access")  for your real needs.

Unless, of course, this is homework, in which case you should learn on your
own time.
```

#### ↳ Re: Using of pointers in HP Micro-Focus COBOL

- **From:** Richard Plinston <riplin@Azonic.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2002-11-17T09:11:21+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DD75D39.76863AE3@Azonic.co.nz>`
- **References:** `<bb7db173.0211160938.1bb60892@posting.google.com>`

```
zis wrote:
 
> I'm interested to get your advise how to use pointers
> in HP Micro-Focus COBOL.
> For example, I would like to create link lists and etc.

'Pointers' are usually raw machine addresses, as used in machine code
and assembly programming.  Cobol is a high level language and avoids
such low-level grubbiness.

However, there is no difficulty in doing linked lists, or trees, or
other structures, just use an array and subscripts or indexes.

      01  LL-Root                   PIC S9(4) VALUE 1.
      01  LL-Limit                  PIC S9(4) VALUE ZERO.
      01  Linked-List-Table.
          03  LL-Item               OCCURS 5000.
           05 LL-Next               PIC S9(4).
           05 LL-Previous           PIC S9(4).
           05 LL-Data ....

The LL-Next and LL-Previous do the linking as if they were 'pointers'
but using these won't corrupt memory or cause a core dump and compiling
with BOUND (or similar) will debug their usage for you.

Of course this is usually a good reason to share data and so most
applications would store data in a file, usually ISAM, rather then keep
it in a private memory structure.
```

#### ↳ Re: I don't understand tables and arrays

- **From:** Donald Tees <Donald_Tees@Sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-11-17T08:55:48-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DD7CA14.6060101@Sympatico.ca>`
- **References:** `<30687b6d.0211161403.7915607b@posting.google.com>`

```
I think your first mistake is definitions.  A table and an array are 
just two different names
for the same thing ... though maybe it could be argued that an array is 
numeric. "Array"
tends to be a mathmatics term, "table" tends to be a business term. 
 Sometimes people
also use "table" to mean an array that is pre-loaded with data. The key 
point is that
the difference is just nuance.  They are the same thing in terms of 
understanding and
writing code.

They are really quite simple ... instead of storing data under the names 
Book1, book2,
book3, etc., you declare one varriable OCCURS several times.  The number 
is the
maximum.  You then refer to them as Book (1) Book (2) Book (3), etc.

The nice thing about doing it that way, is that you can use a variable 
for the subscript.
For example, if you move 2 to the variable SUBSCRIPT-NAME,
then the data reference "Book (SUBSCRIPT-NAME)" is the same as the data
reference "Book (2)".  That allows you to use one piece of code, 
changing the
subscript each time through.

As to your second question, no.  Any data record can be made to occur 
numerous times,
so something like

    01 BOOK-TABLE.
        02 ONE-BOOK        OCCURS 50 TIMES.
            03 BOOK-TITLE            PICTURE X(50).
            03 BOOK-AUTHOR       PICTURE X(50).

contains fifty titles, and fifty authors.  All three data names, (book, 
title and author) need
to be subscripted in code, and any of the three can be used in valid 
statements.

That should at least get you started.

Donald

CodeHead wrote:

>I'm codeing an interactive program, whith the screen section in the
>DATA DIVISION. I have to read in an input file with 12 different
…[18 more quoted lines elided]…

```

Archive-name: computer-lang/cobol-faq
Posting-Frequency: twice a month
Last-Modified: 2002/05/06
Version: 3.03
URL: http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm
Reply-To: wmklein@ix.netcom.com


                            COBOL FAQ
                  (Frequently Asked Questions)

Last-Modified: Spring 2002

The full COBOL FAQ can be viewed at:

  http://www.cobolreport.com/faqs/cobolfaq.htm

If you have comments, corrects, deletions, or additions, please send
private email to:

    wmklein <at> ix.netcom.com

Toward the top of the FAQ is a pointer to the appendix listing the most
recent significant updates and changes.

This FAQ answers such questions as (and addresses topics such as):

 - Where can I get a COBOL compiler? 
 - Is there a free COBOL compiler? 
 - Where can I contact... ?
 - What is happening with the draft of the next COBOL Standard and what
is in it? 
 - What about OO COBOL? 
 - Books about COBOL
 - COBOL Tools
 - What can/should I post in the COBOL newsgroups?
 - What about USAGE? COMP? Storage for data in xyz format? etc?
 - How do I get started with COBOL? Where can I get education?
Tutorials? Etc

and much, MUCH more
```

#### ↳ Re: OT Humour - Hospitals

- **From:** "Charles Hottel" <chottel@cpcug.org>
- **Date:** 2002-11-18T00:01:39+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<01c28e95$a3ff8b20$0ccaf943@chottel>`
- **References:** `<fcd09c56.0211170706.c36585e@posting.google.com>`

```
An 85-year old man went to his doctor's office to get
a sperm count. The 
doctor gave the man a jar and said, "Take this jar
home and bring back a 
semen sample tomorrow."

The next day the 85-year old man reappeared at the
doctor's office and gave 
him the jar, which was as clean and empty as on the
previous day.

The doctor asked what happened and the man explained:
"Well, doc, it's like 
this. First I tried with my right hand, but nothing.
Then I tried with my 
left hand, but still nothing. Then I asked my wife for
help. She tried with 
her right hand, then her left, still nothing. She
tried with her mouth, first 
with the teeth in, then with her teeth out, and still
nothing. We even called 
up Tina, the lady next door and she tried too, first
with both hands, then an 
armpit and she even tried squeezin' it between her
knees, but still nothing." 

The doctor was shocked! "You asked your neighbor?"

The old man replied, "Yep. And no matter what we
tried, we still couldn't get 
the jar open.



Robert Jones <robert@jones0086.freeserve.co.uk> wrote in article
<fcd09c56.0211170706.c36585e@posting.google.com>...

year.

be

…[20 more quoted lines elided]…

pregnant.

might

<a id="t-46fce152bb"></a>
## Thread: Can CA7 scheduler submit a job in my ID? — 1 msgs

_Topics: [`Jobs, careers, recruiting, salary`](topics.md#jobs)_

### Can CA7 scheduler submit a job in my ID?

- **From:** arunprasath420@yahoo.com (Arun Prasath)
- **Date:** 2002-11-17T08:37:22-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<a3ef804e.0211170837.2c90650d@posting.google.com>`

```
Hi all,

Every week I run a few JCLs jobs which inturn produce new JCLs from
templates. These new JCLs update some information about our system
into DB2 tables. The original jobs I run each week are the same, but
the new JCLs are different each week in numbers and content, depending
on the information to be loaded into the DB2 tables.

I currently run all these jobs through my maniframe ID. Inorder to run
the new JCLs produced automatically, I have a REXX program which
attaches a stub at the end of the new JCLs, so that when one JCL
finishes running, it submits the next one inline usign REXX code.

//JOBCARD NEW JCL1
//STEP1
//STEP1
//STEP3
//NEW JCL1's code ends here
//*
//STUB (REXX code which submits the next NEW JCL, in IKJEFT01
environment)

I use 'TSO SUBMIT' in the REXX code to submit the next JCL.

I now need to to schedule these jobs in CA7. I can schedule the
original JCLs, but am not sure how to schedule the dynamically
produced NEW JCLs.

One way I thought of is to schedule the triggering JCL (which is the
STUB itself that submits the first job in the sequence)in CA7 and when
this JCL submits the first NEW JCL, it submits this job in my TSOID,
so that from then on, it looks like the jobs run in my TSOID.

Is this feasible, ie; can CA7 submit a job in my TSOID? instead of
USER=CA7?

Thanks in advance,
Arun Prasath
```

---

<a id="t-16a0cb0e57"></a>
## Thread: C Header file from COBOL copybook — 2 this month / 4 total

_Thread started [2002-08](2002-08.md#t-16a0cb0e57)._

#### ↳ Re: C Header file from COBOL copybook

- **From:** danmeyer <member@mainframeforum.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-18T11:58:40-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3dd91c40$1_6@news.onlynews.com>`
- **References:** `<3Pe39.37706$Jz.7687329@twister.socal.rr.com>`

```
Are you still looking for this? Do you also want accessor and mutator
methods (with code for 88 level edits?) The most difficult aspect is
deriving a language-friendly name from the COBOL based name.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: C Header file from COBOL copybook

- **From:** "Ira Baxter" <idbaxter@semdesigns.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-18T17:21:57-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3dd9749d$1@giga.realtime.net>`
- **References:** `<3Pe39.37706$Jz.7687329@twister.socal.rr.com> <3dd91c40$1_6@news.onlynews.com>`

```
[I'm not sure who started this thread.  Maybe the "mainframe forum" phrase
is a hint that "danmeyer"s message is being reflected here.
Do messages posted to comp.cobol go to mainframe forum, too?]

In any case, the DMS Software Reengineering Toolkit could be used
to do this pretty easily.  It has a full-up COBOL parser,
and can construct C fragments by using source-to-source
transformations.   It could also be used to go the other way.
See http://www.semdesigns.com/Products/DMS/DMSToolkit.html.

I'm not sure I see why deriving a language-friendly name is that hard
an issue.  I'd expect it to be development-system specific, though.
```

## Thread: Cobol Stored Procedures on DB2 — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Databases and SQL`](topics.md#databases)_

### Cobol Stored Procedures on DB2

- **From:** gadana@hotmail.com (SSS)
- **Date:** 2002-11-18T14:22:14-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<c09d2ba1.0211181422.437e2df@posting.google.com>`

```
Current Development Environment:
-----------------------------------------------

- NT, DB2-UDB-v7
- Multiple databases in each database instance
- Stored Procedures are developed in Cobol & deployed as DLLs
- DLLs are placed in C:\Program Files\Sqllib\Function
- Each stored procedure uses more than one program. The main program
issues CALLs to sub-modules.

Everything works like a charm for any one database.

Issue:
-----------------------------------------------

We are not able to have stored procedures for more than one database
per server. As the names of the DLLs have to be the same, we can not
copy the DLLs for the second DB/Version to the same directory where
the first version is stored. We can copy the files to another
directory in PATH. But, then, the other version can not be used.

The only way, we could think of, is to rename all the modules for each
environment. It is not the easiest as we have more than 100 modules.

Does any one of you have any other suggestions? Appreciated.

Siva
```

```
Try cross posting to comp.databases.ibm-db2, there are more people on
that group who are likely to be able to answer you successfully.

Robert

#### ↳ Re: What is Peoplesoft?

- **From:** cmbass_us@yahoo.com (Curtis Bass)
- **Date:** 2002-11-19T08:54:57-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<163715e7.0211190854.11ee8dfc@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<dr0jtu407nclrl9h3464sac9cbskdcbvsq@4ax.com>`

```
Zalek Bloom <ZalekBloom@hotmail.com> wrote in message news:<dr0jtu407nclrl9h3464sac9cbskdcbvsq@4ax.com>...
> Anyone have some idea what is Peoplesoft software? I noticed there is
> demand for this skill.
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> Zalek

Basically, it's Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP) software, like JD
Edwards One World or SAP. What that means is that it's a
comprehensive, integrated software suite which has modules for HRMS,
Benfits, Payroll, G/L, A/P, A/R, etc., all design to scale to large
companies. Typically, they have a high-capacity RDMS on the back end
(Oracle, SQL-Server), and current versions of PeopleSoft are
web-hosted, requiring only a browser on the client.

Curtis
```

## Thread: Xpeditor/cics — 3 msgs

_Topics: [`Mainframe, z/OS, JCL, CICS`](topics.md#mainframe)_

### Xpeditor/cics

- **From:** hpinninti <hpinninti@inautix.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-19T08:14:23+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2062576.1037693663@dbforums.com>`

```

Hi,

   When a cobol-cics online program which is having a COPY SUPPRESS
   statement in it is done xpeditor is is given an error saying that
   SUPPRESS cannot be used in xpeditor.

   I want to know , whether we have any option in xpeditor by which i
   can xped the program with out removing the suppress statement.


  if any one knows how to xpeditor the cics-cobol program having
  suppress plz let me know how is that possible.
```

#### ↳ Re: Xpeditor/cics

- **From:** Robert Graham <rgraham2@nycap.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-19T13:20:15+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DDA3C7E.5070701@nycap.rr.com>`
- **References:** `<2062576.1037693663@dbforums.com>`

```


hpinninti wrote:
> Hi,
> 
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
> Posted via http://dbforums.com


If I remember correctly, Xpeditor needs the copy in order to 
  allow a line by line step through of the probram, allowing 
breakpoints to be set, and data to be seen and/or changed 
for testing purposes.  After you feel the program is safe to 
go into production, then use COPY SUPRESS to supress the 
copy.  Most sites have two sets of compile JCL, one for 
testing which produces a copy for debugging either on 
"paper" or using a utility like Xpeditor.  The other set of 
JCL would be for the production compile and is often set up 
by the systems programmers based upon site requirements.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Xpeditor/cics

- **From:** hpinninti <hpinninti@inautix.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-19T14:39:44+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2063530.1037716784@dbforums.com>`
- **References:** `<2062576.1037693663@dbforums.com> <3DDA3C7E.5070701@nycap.rr.com>`

```

Hi Robert,

    Thanks for your prompt response. I could solve the problem by
    copying the program to my personal PDS and removed the Suppress and
    compiled and then xped.

Once again Thanku very much !


Originally posted by Robert Graham 
> hpinninti wrote:
> > Hi,
…[30 more quoted lines elided]…
> JCL would be for the production compile and is often set up
by the systems programmers based upon site requirements.
```

<a id="t-f02a768439"></a>
## Thread: INVOKE syntax — 3 msgs

### INVOKE syntax

- **From:** hecubas@hotmail.com (Hecubas)
- **Date:** 2002-11-19T09:57:24-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<78337dc.0211190957.40c2e972@posting.google.com>`

```
Hi,

I'm about at my wits end trying to figure out the proper syntax for
Fujitsu Cobol's INVOKE statement.  I've been searching web,
newsgroups, Fujitsu's site and have yet to find anything that might be
useful or up to date.  What I'm doing is taking code that worked with
Microfocus and attempting to get it running in Fujitsu NetCobol 6.1.

Here is the problem child:
      INVOKE WS-XMLDOCUMENT "createElement"
             USING "OLifEs"
             RETURNING WS-NEW-NODE.
Here is the error:
PZOLIFE2.COB 679: JMN3410I-S  BY CONTENT PHRASE CANNOT BE SPECIFIED IN
THE INVOKE STATEMENT,WHEN UNTYPED OBJECT REFERENCE IDENTIFIER,SPECIAL
CLASS OBJECT REFERENCE IDENTIFIER OR SPECIAL CLASS CLASS-NAME
'WS-XMLDOCUMENT' IS SPECIFIED IN INVOKE STATEMENT.

Fujitsu seems to have trouble with having a string literal in the
using clause in this case.

I've tried "USING BY VALUE 'OLifEs'" but that tells me I can't use BY
VALUE.  I've tried moving the string literal to a working storage
field, "USING WS-PROPERTY" and that seems to compile fine, but that's
not the answer I want.

I've tried declaring WS-XMLDOCUMENT as OBJECT REFERENCE COM where COM
is defined the the usual way CLASS COM AS "*COM" and that doesn't
help.

Why oh why doesn't the supplied documentation have anything better
than the "Getting started using COM/ActiveX" pdf?  Even the supplied
language reference doesn't have a proper entry for INVOKE.
```

#### ↳ Re: INVOKE syntax

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-19T12:48:33-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<are124$4u$1@slb6.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<78337dc.0211190957.40c2e972@posting.google.com>`

```
I am *NOT* the best person to answer this, but from the statement about

 "UNTYPED OBJECT REFERENCE IDENTIFIER,SPECIAL  CLASS OBJECT REFERENCE
IDENTIFIER OR SPECIAL CLASS CLASS-NAME  'WS-XMLDOCUMENT' "

in the error message.  I would GUESS that you do need to make this a "typed"
object reference.  Also, what happened when you tried to create a specific
instance of this Object (i.e. INVOKE ... "new")?
```

#### ↳ Re: INVOKE syntax

- **From:** Donald Tees <Donald_Tees@Sympatico.ca>
- **Date:** 2002-11-19T13:30:13-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DDAAD65.2080500@Sympatico.ca>`
- **References:** `<78337dc.0211190957.40c2e972@posting.google.com>`

```
Here is a working stub ...

       IDENTIFICATION DIVISION.
       PROGRAM-ID. "BASE3".
       AUTHOR. D. L. Tees.
       SECURITY.
           Copyright (C), 2002, Donald L. Tees.
       ENVIRONMENT DIVISION.
       CONFIGURATION SECTION.
       REPOSITORY.
           CLASS BASE2.
       SOURCE-COMPUTER. IBM-PC.
       OBJECT-COMPUTER. IBM-PC.
       SPECIAL-NAMES.
           ARGUMENT-NUMBER IS ARG-NO
           ARGUMENT-VALUE  IS ARG-VAL.
       INPUT-OUTPUT SECTION.
       FILE-CONTROL.
       DATA DIVISION.
       FILE SECTION.
       WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
       77 ERROR-NUMBER             PICTURE S999.
       77 NUMBER-OF-ARGUEMENTS     PICTURE 99.
       77 ARGUEMENT-IN             PICTURE X(8).
       77 SMART-OBJECT-ONE         USAGE OBJECT REFERENCE BASE2.
       PROCEDURE DIVISION.
       MAIN-LINE.
           ACCEPT NUMBER-OF-ARGUEMENTS FROM ARG-NO.
           IF NUMBER-OF-ARGUEMENTS IS NOT EQUAL TO 1
               MOVE -999 TO PROGRAM-STATUS
               DISPLAY "BASE3 --> Requires a smart arguement."
               GOBACK.
           ACCEPT ARGUEMENT-IN FROM ARG-VAL.
           IF ARGUEMENT-IN IS NOT EQUAL TO "BASE"
               MOVE -998 TO PROGRAM-STATUS
               DISPLAY "BASE3 --> Requires a smart arguement."
               GOBACK.
           INVOKE BASE2 "NEW" RETURNING SMART-OBJECT-ONE.
           MOVE ARGUEMENT-IN TO SMART-NAME OF SMART-OBJECT-ONE.
           MOVE ERROR-NUMBER OF SMART-OBJECT-ONE TO PROGRAM-STATUS.
           IF PROGRAM-STATUS IS NOT EQUAL TO ZERO
               DISPLAY "BASE3 --> ERROR " PROGRAM-STATUS.
           SET SMART-OBJECT-ONE TO NULL.
           GOBACK.


Hecubas wrote:

>Hi,
>
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
>
I think your problem is that WS-XMLDOCUMENT is not a specific class. 
 Note how I defined
SMART-OBJECT-ONE in the stub above.  You do not need the class if you 
are not going to
use arguements, but if you do, the compiler needs to know what class of 
object it is.

Donald



>Fujitsu seems to have trouble with having a string literal in the
>using clause in this case.
…[17 more quoted lines elided]…
>
```

<a id="t-65263857e6"></a>
## Thread: Indexed Files — 7 this month / 8 total

_Topics: [`VSAM, files, sorting`](topics.md#files)_

### Indexed Files

- **From:** floydj@netins.net (Floyd Johnson)
- **Date:** 2002-11-19T20:10:19+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<are5rb$gfg$1@ins22.netins.net>`

```
We are currently using Fujitsu 6.1 in our File Structures course.  Though 
I have been teaching this course for a number of years, I was caught 
off-guard today by indexed files.  Several questions were raised.

1.  Error 22 in the Fujitsu User Guide gives the following details:  

    "During WRITE or REWRITE statement execution, the value of the prime 
    record key, or alternate record key to be written already exists
    in a file. However, DUPLICATES is specified in the prime record key or 
    alternate record key."

    I thought that if "DUPLICATES" were specified, then there would be no
    error.  Is the User Guide wrong - or am I missing something?

2.  Also, I thought duplicates were not allowed for the primary key.  Is 
    this an extension in Fujitsu COBOL?  I see where it is allowed for 
    relative files, but not indexed files.  How does this work?

Thanks for any follow-up.

Floyd Johnson
```

#### ↳ Re: Indexed Files

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-19T14:18:09-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<are6in$fc9$1@slb5.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<are5rb$gfg$1@ins22.netins.net>`

```
Do you have DUPLICATES specified for the PRIME key or not?  If not, the 22
is EXACTLY the status that you should get.

Micro Focus has had a LONG-time extension of allowing DUPLICATES for PRIME
keys. (definitely NOT standard).  My guess is that Fujitsu also provides
such an extension "for compatibility" - whether they document it or not.

As far as how duplicate prime keys work (at least in Micro Focus) the answer
is BADLY.  They can cause real problems with the semantics of a number of
"standard" COBOL statements.

If your program actually DOES specify "DUPLICATES" for the PRIME key and you
get a 22, then I think there is some sort-of BUG in the Fujitsu product.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Indexed Files

- **From:** floydj@netins.net (Floyd Johnson)
- **Date:** 2002-11-20T04:25:03+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<arf2qv$sng$1@ins22.netins.net>`
- **References:** `<are5rb$gfg$1@ins22.netins.net> <are6in$fc9$1@slb5.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
I always thought the primary key could not have duplicates - and have 
tought it as such.  At least until I saw the description for Status Code 
22 posted in my earlier message.  That description was copy and pasted 
directly from the Fujitsu User Guide for version 6.1.  I suspect it was 
also in verison 3.0 (provided on the old free CDs from four or five years 
back) since I have a hand out based on that earlier version where I list 
all the Status Code.  

The Status Code description suggests that code 22 occurs when "DUPLICATES" 
are specified for the primary or secondary keys and I have reused a key 
value. I think that is wrong.  The error code should occur when 
"DUPLICATES" is not specified and I am entering a new record with the 
same key value. 

This concurs with what you have said.  On the other hand, I find it 
surprising that no one has reported an error in the Fujitsu manual between 
versions 3.0 and 6.1.  I just wanted to make sure I had not missed 
something in the process.   

Thank you for your comments.

Floyd Johnson

#### ↳ Re: Indexed Files

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2002-11-19T20:53:41-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0211192053.567bfc25@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<are5rb$gfg$1@ins22.netins.net>`

```
floydj@netins.net (Floyd Johnson) wrote 

> 1.  Error 22 in the Fujitsu User Guide gives the following details:  
> 
…[6 more quoted lines elided]…
>     error.  Is the User Guide wrong - or am I missing something?

If you write a record that is a duplicate in a key that allows
duplicates you will get a file-status of '02'.  The first character of
'0' indicates that the operation completed successfully, the 2nd
character or '2' indicates a duplicate key.  This status also occurs
on a read where there are multiples of one of the keys.

Ensure that you only check the first character for '0' as success and
not both characters as '00'.

If the write failed because a duplicate key was not allowed the status
will be '22'.

The User Guide has not been edited very well and it seems they
cut-and-paste from the '02' to the '22' without taking off the
'however'.


> 2.  Also, I thought duplicates were not allowed for the primary key.  Is 
>     this an extension in Fujitsu COBOL?  I see where it is allowed for 
>     relative files, but not indexed files.  How does this work?

Duplicate primary keys on an indexed file are possible as an
extension, but would be a real pain.

For a relative file the 'primary key' is the record number, I can't
see how duplicates could be anything other than impossible.
```

- **From:** "JamesD" <mangogrower@telocity.com>

```
In my experince, some database systems WILL allow a file with a NON-
Unique key.  However, COBOL on those systems can NOT process the file.
The manual is incorrect.
What good would a 'non-unique' Primary key be?

no

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Indexed Files

- **From:** lsunley@mb.sympatico.ca
- **Date:** 2002-11-28T02:27:23+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<ZpzG4UNLyRNq-pn2-LJnHuOV6lCWR@h24-82-204-17.wp.shawcable.net>`
- **References:** `<are5rb$gfg$1@ins22.netins.net> <217e491a.0211192053.567bfc25@posting.google.com> <3de5777b$1_4@nopics.sjc>`

```
The Microfocus file handler allows for duplicate primary keys and it 
works.

Normally you would use it to group a set of records in an indexed file
that you would process as a set. Do a read on the primary key and if 
you get a "0/2" file status you do a read next until you see a 
different key.

That way you can access a single key file that provides for a group of
related records.

Lorne

## Thread: Find the length of a character field — 11 this month / 12 total

### Find the length of a character field

- **From:** "Thierry Lespagnard" <fa292741@skynet.be>
- **Date:** 2002-11-20T00:09:18+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3ddac473$0$203$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be>`

```
I want to know the length of a field (from the start of a field to the last
non-blank character). I use "perform varying" but the performances are bad.
Any idea to improve this ?

Thanks.
```

#### ↳ Re: Find the length of a character field

- **From:** Robert Graham <rgraham2@nycap.rr.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-19T23:40:16+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DDACDB8.1000402@nycap.rr.com>`
- **References:** `<3ddac473$0$203$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be>`

```


Thierry Lespagnard wrote:
> I want to know the length of a field (from the start of a field to the last
> non-blank character). I use "perform varying" but the performances are bad.
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> 


When you say "perofrm varying" are you starting from the 
first character (varying y from 1 by 1 until greater than x) 
or from the end (varying y from x by -1 until y equal zero)? 
  Starting from the end might work better.  I am assuming 
that this is a string input "field" of varying lengths.  You 
would probable have to know the max possible length.  On the 
other hand if it is one "record" with multiple fields 
deliminated by space then you don't have much choice, you 
start at the beginning.  You'd use a working-storage field 
to track the "length", refreshing it after field length has 
been determined.
```

- **From:** "Terry Heinze" <terryheinze@prodigy.net>
- **Date:** 2002-11-21T04:56:54+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<qIZC9.5402$Fy5.139139226@newssvr15.news.prodigy.com>`
- **References:** `<3ddac473$0$203$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be> <9SAC9.3416$tY1.60998821@newssvr17.news.prodigy.com> <arg7fo$mt0$1@peabody.colorado.edu>`

microseconds longer or shorter.  The INSPECT is simply less wordy and more
readable (to me).  If I had tons of these, I would probably do a benchmark
comparing the INSPECT against a PERFORM VARYING.  My code looked almost
identical to the post by the person who also used the INSPECT with the
FUNCTION REVERSE option.

#### ↳ Re: Find the length of a character field

- **From:** "Ron Schnatzmeyer" <NoSoy@swbell.net>
- **Date:** 2002-11-19T20:25:16-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<arerpr$i4b$1@nntp-m01.news.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<3ddac473$0$203$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be>`

```
> I want to know the length of a field (from the start of a field to the last
> non-blank character). I use "perform varying" but the performances are bad.
> Any idea to improve this ?
>
> Thanks.
--------------------------------------------

05  field-1 pic x(20) value 'abcde'.
05  tc      pic 99    value zero.
05  chr-len pic 99.

inspect function reverse (field-1) tallying tc for leading spaces.
compute chr-len = length of field-1 - tc.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Find the length of a character field

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.ix.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2002-11-19T21:38:50-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<arf04m$o4$1@slb9.atl.mindspring.net>`
- **References:** `<3ddac473$0$203$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be> <arerpr$i4b$1@nntp-m01.news.aol.com>`

```
Personally, I think that the INSPECT with FUNCTION REVERSE is the most (to
me) self-documenting.  But I believe that in most systems where CLC people
have run tests, a PERFORM VARYING *performs* better. (Also if you are
running with a pre-Intrinsic Function compiler, you will need to use PERFORM
VARYING). We have had this discussion several times in the past and you
might want to check out the archives (google?) for performance reports.

As far as the other note asking about whether it is "faster" to PERFORM
VARYING from the beginning or the end of the string, I would *guess* that
would depend on whether the majority of strings "searched" had more than
half or less than half of the "field" filled.  OTOH, if you are searching
from the beginning you need to "compare" the entire rest of the string
(reference modification) for spaces not to fall into an "intermediate" space
causing you to end the PERFORM while if you start at the end, you just need
to stop when you FIRST hit a single space character.  Depending on the
compiler (generated machine code) and operating system, that might be faster
even if most of the strings are less than half filled.

To "recapitulate" (and I am typing these in by hand - so compiler errors -
or even logic errors - might occur)

NOTE WELL:
   Others in CLC *may* have "tweaks" to the following to optimize each of
the approaches.  I haven't tested them, so there might be some better coding
techniques - especially depending on what optimizations your compiler can
do.

For:

 01  Variable-Length-String
        05  EachByte     Occurs 1 to 300 times
                                   Depending on SomeThing
                                   Pic X.
*> if your string  is smaller than 255, your results MIGHT be better.
 01  End-Results    Pic 9(03).        *> using a sign or USAGE
BINARY/COMP-5/whatever might help
 01  Temp-Num1    Pic 9(03).        *> using a sign or USAGE
BINARY/COMP-5/whatever might help

Option-1 (probably a medium-poor performer - but easy to understand)

    Move Zero to Temp-Num1
    Inspect function Reverse (String-to-Test)
         Tallying Temp-Num1 for leading spaces.
     Compute End-Result = (Function Length (Variable-Length-String)) -
Temp-Num1.
* You could use SomeThing instead of "Function Length
(Variable-Length-String)
*   If that really DOES include the current string length

Option-2  (possibly the BEST performing on many systems)

    Move Zero to End-Result
    Perform
       Varying Temp-Num1
          From Something by -1
          Until Temp-Num1 < 1
                If String-to-Test ((Temp-Num1 - 1) : 1) Not = Space
                    Move Temp-Num1 to End-Result
                    Move 1 to Temp-Num1
               End-If
    End-Perform
    If End-Result = Zero
        Move SomeThing to End-Result
    End-If

Option-3 (Might perform best if the majority of strings are less than half
used)

    Move Zero to End-Result
    Perform
       Varying Temp-Num1
          From by +1 by +1
          Until Temp-Num1 >= Something
                If String-to-Test (Temp-Num1 : )  = Space
                    Subtract 1 from Temp-Num1 Giving End-Result
                    Move SomeThing  to Temp-Num1
               End-If
    End-Perform
    If End-Result = Zero
        Move SomeThing to End-Result
    End-If
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Find the length of a character field

- **From:** "Ron Schnatzmeyer" <NoSoy@swbell.net>
- **Date:** 2002-11-20T19:40:20-06:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<arhdhh$id9$1@nntp-m01.news.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<3ddac473$0$203$ba620e4c@news.skynet.be> <arerpr$i4b$1@nntp-m01.news.aol.com> <arf04m$o4$1@slb9.atl.mindspring.net>`

```
There are a number of ways to do this with perform varying but
I find this to be the most straightforward, well to me at least.
At the end of the perform data-name dl contains the length of
the data string. Everything is done in the perform, no additional
if's.


05 dl  pic 99.  (dl = data length)
05 field-1 pic x(20) value 'abcde'.

perform varying dl from length of field-1 by -1
 until field-1 (dl:1) not = space or dl = zero
   continue
end-perform.
```

#### ↳ Re: Find the length of a character field

- **From:** Colin Campbell <cmcampb@attglobal.net>
- **Date:** 2002-11-20T11:42:43-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3DDBE5B3.36031519@attglobal.net>`

How about using UNSTRING ... DELIMITED ALL SPACE INTO ... COUNT ...?

This might not be a super performer, either, but you would have the advantage
of not only getting the character count, but isolating the string in another
variable.  Of course, it isn't going to work too well if you have leading
spaces....
